title: Define v1 scenario surface, unsupported-field policy, and config migration
description:
Define the exact v1 scenario/customization surface before model derivation, CLI override handling, or TUI field metadata expands further.

This should enumerate supported fields, explicitly list unsupported or future fields, define how unsupported fields fail validation, and define config versioning/migration behavior for existing config.yaml and future override files.

## Decision
Create a bounded v1 scenario surface as the contract for model derivation, CLI overrides, and TUI field metadata. Unsupported fields must be rejected or explicitly quarantined according to the documented compatibility policy rather than silently mapped into model constants.

## Rationale
The scenario system crosses multiple implementation tracks. A shared surface prevents late discovery of new knobs, config/TUI contract churn, and accidental exposure of unstable internals as user-facing configuration.

## Alternatives Considered
1. Let each implementation bead discover supported fields as needed. This maximizes local momentum but creates cross-surface drift.
2. Support arbitrary fields and validate later in the model. This is flexible but makes deterministic behavior and migration hard to reason about.
3. Freeze a small v1 surface with explicit unsupported-field behavior. This is the preferred path because it keeps implementation bounded while leaving room for documented future expansion.
design:
# V1 Scenario Surface Definition

## Supported V1 Fields

The v1 scenario surface is the set of config.yaml fields that model derivation, CLI overrides, TUI editing, and validation may act on. Fields are grouped by domain.

### Top-Level

| Field | Type | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| seed | int | 42 | Global PRNG seed. ScenarioContext derives per-domain seeds from this. |
| output_dir | str | "test_suite" | Output root. |

### Company

| Field | Type | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| company.name | str | "Cascade Industries, Inc." | Legal name used in all generated documents. |
| company.type | str | "US C-Corporation" | Entity type label. |
| company.industry | str | "Mid-market manufacturer" | Free-text descriptor. |
| company.headquarters | str | "Portland, Oregon" | HQ city/state. |
| company.fiscal_year_end | str | "12-31" | MM-DD format. |
| company.years | list[int] | [2023, 2024, 2025] | Fiscal years covered. Must be sorted ascending. |
| company.current_year | int | 2025 | Active reporting year; must be in years list. |
| company.consolidated_revenue | int | 200_000_000 | Total top-line revenue in dollars. |

### Subsidiaries (company.subsidiaries.<key>)

Each subsidiary key is a stable identifier (e.g. precision_components).

| Field | Type | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| legal_name | str | — | Required. |
| location | str | — | Required. City, State format. |
| state | str | — | Required. Two-letter state code. |
| entity_code | str | — | Required. Short code for ledger prefixing. |
| revenue | int | — | Required. Subsidiary-level revenue. |
| type | str | — | Required. Free-text business description. |
| gross_margin | float | — | Required. 0.0–1.0. |
| employee_count | int | — | Required. Headcount for this subsidiary. |
| rd_spend_pct | float | 0.0 | Optional. R&D spend as fraction of revenue. |

### Growth Rates (company.growth_rates)

| Field | Type | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| fy2023_to_fy2024 | float | 0.06 | Year-over-year growth rate. |
| fy2024_to_fy2025 | float | 0.09 | Year-over-year growth rate. |

Note: growth rate field names are tied to the years list. Future expansion may generalize this to a list of {from_year, to_year, rate} tuples; v1 keeps the current fixed keys.

### Intercompany (company.intercompany)

| Field | Type | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| raw_materials_markup | float | 0.08 | Cost-plus percentage for intercompany transfers. |
| management_fee_pct | float | 0.015 | Parent management fee as fraction of sub revenue. |
| intercompany_loan_principal | int | 5_000_000 | Outstanding intercompany loan amount. |
| intercompany_loan_rate | float | 0.05 | Interest rate on intercompany loan. |

### Employees (company.employees)

| Field | Type | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| total_count | int | 850 | Consolidated headcount. |
| annual_turnover_rate | float | 0.08 | Annual attrition rate. |
| remote_states | list[str] | ["CA", "WA", "NY"] | States with remote employees for payroll tax. |

### Seasonal Weights (company.seasonal_weights)

| Field | Type | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Q1 | float | 0.20 | Must sum to 1.0 across all quarters. |
| Q2 | float | 0.25 | |
| Q3 | float | 0.25 | |
| Q4 | float | 0.30 | |

### Build-Time Populated (not user-editable in v1)

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| canary_assignments | dict | Populated by canary generator. |
| error_injections | dict | Populated by error framework. |

## Explicitly Unsupported Fields in V1

The following are known future candidates but are NOT part of the v1 surface. They must not appear in config.yaml, CLI overrides, or TUI field metadata.

| Candidate | Reason Deferred |
|---|---|
| Product lines (names, growth rates, shares) | Hardcoded in revenue.py; requires formatter refactor to derive from config. |
| Department structure / job titles / salary ranges | Hardcoded in employees.py; complex cross-referential impact. |
| Opex categories and entity-specific rates | Hardcoded in opex.py; high coupling with ledger structure. |
| Legal contracts / clauses / amendments | Canonical tuples in legal.py; diligence pack owns these as model facts. |
| HR diligence data (employment agreements, retention, severance) | Canonical tuples in hr_diligence.py; pack-owned model facts. |
| Fixed asset definitions | Hardcoded in assets module. |
| Bank model parameters | Hardcoded in bank.py. |
| Difficulty level / error density | No spec support yet; future customization track. |
| Scenario pack selection in config.yaml | Packs are selected via CLI --packs flag (synth-data-ups.5), not config file. |
| Per-test-case overrides | Future TUI/customization feature (synth-data-2u6 epic). |

## Unsupported-Field Policy

### Validation behavior

When config.yaml or an override file contains a field that is not in the v1 supported surface:

1. **Top-level unknown keys**: Reject with ConfigError listing the unknown keys. This prevents typos from silently producing default behavior.
2. **Unknown keys within a supported section** (e.g. company.foo): Reject with ConfigError.
3. **Unknown keys within subsidiaries**: Reject with ConfigError. Only the fields listed above are accepted.

### Policy rationale

Strict rejection is preferred over warn-and-ignore because:
- Silent ignoring masks typos (e.g. "employes" vs "employees").
- Config drift between what users think they configured and what the generator uses is the #1 source of confusion in deterministic generators.
- Adding a new field is a deliberate v2 surface expansion, not an accidental omission.

### Override files (future)

When layered override files are introduced (synth-data-2u6 track), they follow the same policy: only v1 surface fields may be overridden. Unknown fields in an override file are rejected at load time with the same ConfigError.

## Config Versioning and Migration

### V1 stance

- **No version marker in v1.** The current config.yaml has no version field and works. Adding a mandatory version field would break existing configs for no gain.
- **Future version field**: When the surface expands (v2), a `config_version: 2` field will be added. Configs without a version field are treated as v1.
- **Migration path**: When v2 arrives, the loader will detect missing version field → apply v1 defaults for any new fields → emit a one-time deprecation warning suggesting the user add `config_version: 2`.

### Existing config.yaml compatibility

The current config.yaml is a valid v1 config. No changes are needed to it. The v1 surface was defined to match exactly what config.yaml already contains (minus canary_assignments and error_injections, which are build-time populated and not user-editable).

### CLI --config behavior

`--config path/to/config.yaml` loads and validates against the v1 surface. The flag's behavior does not change.

## Examples

### Accepted config (minimal custom)

```yaml
seed: 99
output_dir: custom_suite
company:
  name: "Acme Corp"
  type: "US C-Corporation"
  industry: "SaaS"
  headquarters: "San Francisco, CA"
  fiscal_year_end: "12-31"
  years: [2024, 2025]
  current_year: 2025
  consolidated_revenue: 50_000_000
  subsidiaries:
    main:
      legal_name: "Acme Corp"
      location: "San Francisco, CA"
      state: "CA"
      entity_code: "AC"
      revenue: 50_000_000
      type: "SaaS platform"
      gross_margin: 0.72
      employee_count: 200
  growth_rates:
    fy2023_to_fy2024: 0.15
    fy2024_to_fy2025: 0.20
  intercompany:
    raw_materials_markup: 0.0
    management_fee_pct: 0.0
    intercompany_loan_principal: 0
    intercompany_loan_rate: 0.0
  employees:
    total_count: 200
    annual_turnover_rate: 0.12
    remote_states: ["NY", "TX"]
  seasonal_weights:
    Q1: 0.25
    Q2: 0.25
    Q3: 0.25
    Q4: 0.25
canary_assignments: {}
error_injections: {}
```

### Rejected config (unsupported field)

```yaml
seed: 42
output_dir: test_suite
difficulty: hard          # ← REJECTED: unknown top-level key
company:
  name: "Cascade Industries, Inc."
  product_lines:          # ← REJECTED: unknown key in company section
    - name: "Widgets"
  ...
```

Error:
```
ConfigError: Unknown top-level key(s): {'difficulty'}
ConfigError: Unknown key(s) in 'company': {'product_lines'}
```
acceptance_criteria: Design records supported v1 fields, unsupported-field behavior, config version marker or migration stance, compatibility expectations for existing config.yaml, and examples of accepted and rejected override shapes.
close_reason: Decision recorded. V1 surface covers: seed, output_dir, and all company.* fields currently in config.yaml (company params, subsidiaries, growth rates, intercompany, employees, seasonal weights). canary_assignments and error_injections are build-time only. Unsupported fields (product lines, departments, salaries, legal/HR tuples, assets, bank, difficulty, pack selection, per-TC overrides) are explicitly enumerated and rejected with ConfigError. No version marker in v1; future v2 will add config_version field with automatic v1 fallback. Existing config.yaml is a valid v1 config with no changes needed.

title: Make normal grading fail when agent structured data is absent
description:
TestCaseGrader._load_agent_data falls back to self._expected. That is only valid for SelfTestGrader. Normal grading should return None/no-data and score correctness as 1 with clear detail.

## Steps to Reproduce
Run normal grading for a test case with gold data present but with no agent JSON or extractable structured output. The current normal grader can fall back to expected data and compare the gold standard to itself.

## Expected Behavior
Normal grading treats absent or unparseable agent structured data as no-data and fails correctness with a clear detail message. SelfTestGrader remains allowed to use expected data as its synthetic agent response.

## Fix Direction
Move the expected-data fallback behind the SelfTestGrader override and make the base TestCaseGrader return no usable agent data when the response cannot be loaded.
acceptance_criteria: Normal grader no-output/no-JSON case fails correctness; SelfTestGrader override still returns expected data.
close_reason: Fixed: base TestCaseGrader._load_agent_data() no longer falls back to self._expected when no agent JSON is found — returns None instead, causing correctness to score 1. SelfTestGrader override unchanged. All 386 tests pass, self-test 18/18 at 3/3/3/3/3.

title: Fix real-grading canary verification to search only agent outputs
description:
In scoring/auto_grader.py TestCaseGrader.verify_canaries searches agent output and suite input_files. Normal grading must search only agent outputs; SelfTestGrader keeps input-file search.

## Steps to Reproduce
Run normal grading against a fixture where a canary appears only in generated suite input files and not in the agent output. The current real-grading path can pass the canary check by reading benchmark inputs instead of requiring evidence from the agent response.

## Expected Behavior
Normal grading searches only agent outputs for canaries. SelfTestGrader remains the only path allowed to search generated input files to validate benchmark embedding.

## Fix Direction
Split real-grading evidence lookup from self-test evidence lookup so the normal grader cannot over-credit canaries from input files.

title: Tech stack decision: Python 3.11+ with pinned office-file libraries
description:
Language: Python 3.11+ (spec mandates it in §2.1/§2.2).

Pinned stack:
- openpyxl — xlsx read/write (formulas, merged cells, formatting)
- python-docx — docx (styles, headers, tables)
- reportlab — text-native PDFs
- fpdf2 — scanned-style PDFs (low-res image output)
- pypdf — PDF read-back for auto-grader
- pandas + numpy — financial data generation, 52K-row AP ledger, Benford analysis
- faker — synthetic names/addresses (with fixed seed)
- pyyaml — config.yaml
- pytest — generator + grader tests
- uv (or poetry) — lockfile for reproducibility

Determinism rules (required by §9 quality gate: byte-identical reruns):
- random.seed(42) + numpy.random.default_rng(42)
- Avoid unordered set iteration; sort dict keys before writing
- Faker: Faker.seed(42) and per-instance seeding
- Lock all dependency versions via lockfile

PDF DETERMINISM GOTCHA: reportlab embeds timestamps and producer strings by default, which breaks byte-identical reruns. Must set invariant=True on Canvas and pin a fixed creation date in document metadata. fpdf2 has a similar issue — set creation_date and modification_date explicitly. Verify this early by running the generator twice and diffing the PDF outputs; do not wait until the §9 quality gate to discover it.
close_reason: Decision recorded. Stack is locked: Python 3.11+, openpyxl/python-docx/reportlab/fpdf2/pypdf/pandas/numpy/faker/pyyaml/pytest/uv. Determinism rules and PDF timestamp gotcha documented in description for reference by dependent beads.

  2. Grader Instructions (per-dimension, per-TC guidance)

  4. Aggregate Dashboard — capability matrix pivots on 3 axes:

title: TC-23: Swiss Multi-Currency Bank Reconciliation (CHF/EUR/USD)
description:
Design complete, reviewed by advocate/devil's advocate. APPROVED WITH CHANGES.

SERVICE LINE: Audit | DIFFICULTY: Complex | JURISDICTION: CH (Zürich)
ENTITY: Cascade Precision Instruments AG — 3 UBS accounts (CHF/EUR/USD)

WHAT IT TESTS: Multi-currency bank reconciliation (275 transactions across 3 currencies), FX revaluation verification against SNB rates, SNB rate convention handling (foreign currency per 1 CHF — must invert), German-language bank statements with DTA/LSV+ payment format parsing, Swiss bank confirmation (single letter covering all accounts), consolidated cash position in CHF.

DIFFERENTIATOR FROM TC-02: TC-02 is single-currency USD. TC-23 adds three currency pools, FX revaluation verification, SNB rate convention, Swiss payment formats, and a consolidated multi-currency cash position.

INPUT FILES (6): cpi_bank_statement_chf_dec2025.csv (185 txns), cpi_bank_statement_eur_dec2025.csv (62 txns), cpi_bank_statement_usd_dec2025.csv (28 txns), cpi_gl_cash_dec2025.xlsx (3 sheets), cpi_bank_confirmations_fy2025.pdf, snb_fx_rates_dec2025.csv

PLANTED ERRORS:
- ERR-023-001 (stale_fx_rate): EUR account month-end revaluation uses FY2024 closing rate 0.9285 (1.0770 CHF/EUR) instead of correct FY2025 rate 0.9387 (1.0653 CHF/EUR), overstating by CHF 6,324

GOLD STANDARD: CHF adjusted CHF 2,817,905; EUR adjusted EUR 1,186,650 = CHF 1,264,027; USD adjusted USD 474,550 = CHF 536,578; consolidated CHF 4,618,510

REVIEW REQUIRED CHANGES:
1. Pin down exact EUR balance subject to revaluation so CHF 6,324 impact is arithmetically exact (currently implies EUR ~540,720 which yields CHF 6,326)
2. Rename entity code from CZ to CP or PI for naming convention consistency

Full design at /tmp/design_tc01ch_tc02ch.md
close_reason: Implemented TC-23: Swiss Multi-Currency Bank Reconciliation. Created generator/model/bank_ch.py (Swiss bank model with 3 UBS accounts CHF/EUR/USD, 275 transactions, gold standard reconciliation values computed from SNB rates), generator/formatters/tc23.py (emits 6 input files + prompt + expected behavior + gold standard), generator/packs/ch_variants.py (new CH pack). ERR-CH-002 planted: EUR revaluation uses stale FY2024 rate 0.9285 instead of 0.9387, CHF 13,887 overstatement. Gold values are mathematically exact from SNB rates (adjusted from design draft estimates). Determinism verified, all tests pass, ruff clean.

BLOCKING REVIEW FINDINGS:

title: TC-31: Swiss Pension Fund (BVG) Audit — Funding Ratio & Reserve Analysis
description:
Design complete, reviewed by advocate/devil's advocate. APPROVED WITH CHANGES — has one factual error to fix.

SERVICE LINE: Audit | DIFFICULTY: Complex | JURISDICTION: CH (Zürich)
ENTITY: Pensionskasse der Cascade Precision Instruments AG — autonomous BVG fund

WHAT IT TESTS: Swiss GAAP FER 26 pension fund accounting, Deckungsgrad (funding ratio) calculation and adequacy assessment, Wertschwankungsreserve (fluctuation reserve) target vs actual, investment performance benchmarking against Pictet BVG-25 Plus, contribution reconciliation to payroll, risk benefit reserve adequacy, OAK BV regulatory compliance.

GENUINELY NEW: No existing TC covers pension fund audits. Not a variant — entirely new domain.

INPUT FILES (8): pensionskasse_jahresrechnung.xlsx, aktuarielles_gutachten.pdf, anlageperformance.xlsx, beitragsabrechnung.xlsx, risikoversicherung.xlsx, oak_bv_compliance_checklist.xlsx, lohnliste.csv, stiftungsratsbeschluss.pdf

PLANTED ERRORS:
- ERR-031-001 (stale_data): Actuarial report uses technischer Zinssatz 2.00% (stale) — correct rate should be lower. Impact: ~CHF 2.1M understatement of provisions, funding ratio overstated from 107.16% to ~102.7% corrected
- ERR-031-002 (mismatched_total): December employer contribution total CHF 287,450 vs sum of individuals CHF 284,950 (terminated employee 0089 not removed)

GOLD STANDARD: Plan assets CHF 52.4M; provisions CHF 48.9M; reported funding ratio 107.16%; corrected ~102.7%; WSR shortfall CHF 4.36M; fund return 5.8% vs benchmark 6.2%

CRITICAL REVIEW FINDING — MUST FIX:
The stale rate of 2.00% is labeled as BVG-Mindestzinssatz 2023, but the actual BVG minimum rate in 2023 was 1.00% (not 2.00%). MUST relabel as the fund's own technischer Zinssatz (actuarial discount rate) which is distinct from the BVG minimum rate. Narrative: actuary recommended reducing from 2.00% to 1.50% but board hasn't updated.

OTHER CHANGES:
1. Reduce headcount to 110-120 employees for Swiss manufacturing realism
2. Specify Pictet BVG index variant (BVG-25 Plus confirmed in design)
3. Full design at /tmp/design_tc17eu_tc19ch.md

WHAT IT TESTS: Multi-entity EBITDA consolidation with IC eliminations, 10 management adjustment evaluations with European regulatory context (Kurzarbeit, CIR, furlough, works council), hidden liabilities (Betriebsrat Sozialplan EUR 400-600K), Pillar Two scoping (judgment trap — group below EUR 750M threshold), EUR/GBP translation sensitivity.

DIFFERENTIATOR FROM TC-11: TC-11 is single-entity US GAAP QofE. TC-26 adds 4-entity consolidation, IC elimination verification, European-specific government subsidies, works council hidden liabilities, Pillar Two assessment.

INPUT FILES (6): monthly_pl_by_entity.xlsx, management_adjustments.xlsx, customer_contracts/ (10 PDFs), management_interview_notes.docx, ic_elimination_schedule.xlsx, statutory_accounts_summary.pdf

PLANTED ERRORS:
- ERR-026-001 (mismatched_total): IC elimination schedule double-counts CE management fees (EUR 2,840K vs correct EUR 1,470K)
- ERR-026-002 (stale_data): CP Gewerbesteuer rate 29.58% uses old Hebesatz 480% instead of 490% (correct 29.90%)

GOLD STANDARD: Reported EBITDA EUR 14.8M; adjusted EBITDA ~EUR 15.9M; challenge adjustments #6 (Airbus run-rate), #8 (recurring maintenance), #9 (recurring customs), #10 (works council); Pillar Two NOT applicable

REVIEW REQUIRED CHANGES:
1. Acknowledge ERR-026-002 parallel with existing ERR-EU-018 (same Gewerbesteuer issue)
2. Fix approver anomaly in TC-27 gold standard (CE Prokura is EUR 25K, not EUR 10K)
3. Full design at /tmp/design_tc11eu_tc13eu.md

title: TC-25: European Multi-Jurisdiction AR Workpaper with IFRS 9 ECL
description:
Design complete, reviewed by advocate/devil's advocate. APPROVED WITH CHANGES.

SERVICE LINE: Audit | DIFFICULTY: Complex | JURISDICTION: NL/DE/FR/UK
ENTITIES: CE (NL), CP (DE), CM (FR), CD (UK) — consolidated AR ~EUR 20M

WHAT IT TESTS: IFRS 9 expected credit loss (ECL) simplified approach with provision matrix, EU Late Payment Directive 2011/7/EU compliance, multi-jurisdiction confirmation practices (FR negative confirmations, DE low response rates), multi-currency AR (EUR/GBP), jurisdiction concentration risk.

title: auto_grader gives perfect correctness score when zero comparisons made
description: scoring/auto_grader.py:326-328 — When total==0 (no numerical values to compare), the grader assigns score 3 (perfect). A malformed gold standard with all string values when they should be numbers silently gets a perfect correctness score. No warning is emitted when zero comparisons are made despite expected_outputs being non-empty. Fix: Emit warning when total==0 but expected_outputs is non-empty, or score as 1 (unknown).
close_reason: Fixed: When total==0 but expected_outputs is non-empty, grader now scores 1 (unknown) and emits a warning + fail check instead of silently scoring 3. Empty expected_outputs still correctly scores 3. Added two regression tests.

title: Config-driven revenue generation broken for non-default entity codes
description: generator/model/revenue.py:321-327 and 234-237 — generate_monthly_revenue does not pass entity_growth_fy24_to_fy25 from config, always using hardcoded dict with keys PC/AM/DS. The hardcoded PRODUCT_LINES tuple (lines 80-141) also references specific entity codes. Custom entity codes via entities_from_config will KeyError on line 240. config.company.growth_rates.fy2024_to_fy2025 field exists but is never consumed. The entire config-driven revenue path is broken for non-default entities.
close_reason: Fixed: (1) Pass fy2024_to_fy2025 growth rate from config to _build_annual_table. (2) Add _adapt_product_lines() to remap PRODUCT_LINES entity codes (PC/AM/DS) to config entity codes via subsidiary key matching. (3) Make _build_annual_table accept product_lines parameter. (4) Use adapted product lines in generate_monthly_revenue main loop. Added 3 tests verifying custom entity codes (PRC/ADM/DST) don't KeyError and produce correct output.

title: tc16.py uses bare relative path for engagement letter template
description: generator/formatters/tc16.py:488 — Path('templates/engagement_letter_template.docx') resolves against cwd, not project root. If generator is invoked from any directory other than project root (CI runner, parent dir), this raises FileNotFoundError. Other formatters resolve relative to output_dir or Path(__file__). Fix: Resolve relative to project root or Path(__file__).parent.
close_reason: Fixed: Resolved template path relative to __file__ (repo root) instead of bare relative path. Follows established pattern from tc05.py.

title: validate_model() never called during actual generation
description: generator/model/validation.py — validate_model() is only called from tests/test_model_validation.py. The main orchestrator (generate_test_suite.py) does not invoke it after build_model(). Model invariant checks (GL balance, revenue tolerance, IC netting) only run during testing, never during actual generation. A user running generate_test_suite.py could produce invalid output without warning. Fix: Call validate_model() from generate() or document validation is test-only.
close_reason: Already resolved: validate_model() is called at generate_test_suite.py:87 after build_model() at line 84. It raises RuntimeError with categorized messages if any invariants fail. All 9 test_model_validation tests pass.

title: 4 formatters import hardcoded ENTITIES instead of reading from CascadeModel
description: tc07.py:32, tc09.py:62, tc10.py:44, tc12.py:73 import ENTITIES/SUBSIDIARIES directly from generator.model.entities rather than using model.entities from the CascadeModel passed to them. When a user provides custom config that changes subsidiary names, these formatters silently use hardcoded Cascade Industries defaults, producing inconsistent output. Fix: Read entity data from model.entities/model.subsidiaries.

title: _FIXED_DATETIME has 3 different values across 26 formatter files
description: Most formatters use datetime(2025, 3, 15, 9, 0, 0), but tc04.py/tc04_eu.py use datetime(2025, 1, 15, 9, 0, 0), tc14.py uses datetime(2025, 3, 28, 9, 0, 0), and templates.py uses datetime(2025, 1, 15, 9, 0, 0). The shared writers/__init__.py defines the canonical FIXED_DATETIME but nobody except tc19-21 uses it. If the divergence is intentional it should be documented; if not it silently breaks determinism assumptions.
close_reason: Fixed: Standardized all _FIXED_DATETIME and related tuple timestamps to canonical datetime(2025, 3, 15, 9, 0, 0) across tc04.py, tc04_eu.py, tc14.py, and templates.py. Also fixed two inline tuple-form dates in tc04.py and tc04_eu.py _save_xlsx_deterministic functions.

title: _save_xlsx/docx_deterministic copy-pasted across 25+ formatters
description: The function _save_xlsx_deterministic is defined identically in 25 separate formatter files. Similarly _save_docx_deterministic is duplicated in 18 files. A shared version already exists in generator/writers/__init__.py as save_xlsx_deterministic and save_docx_deterministic, but only tc19/tc20/tc21 use it. Each local copy also redeclares _FIXED_DATETIME and _FIXED_ZIP_DT. Bug fixes must be applied to 25+ files independently. Fix: Delete per-file copies and import from generator.writers.

title: auto_grader._values_match() has no direct unit tests
description: scoring/auto_grader.py:232-261 — _values_match() is the core numerical comparison function for all correctness scoring. Handles int/float tolerance, string comparison, nested list/dict recursion, and zero-value edge case. No test exercises it directly. Edge cases untested: zero expected value (abs_tol branch line 242), mixed-type comparison (line 261 fallback), nested dict with missing keys (line 255), and 0.5% tolerance boundary.

title: test_fidelity_report.py silently skips AR tieout assertion
description: tests/test_fidelity_report.py:84-88 — test_ar_invoice_receipt_tieout guards its assertion behind 'if ar_invoices.total in report and ar_receipts.total in report'. Since fidelity_report.py conditionally populates these keys, the test silently passes when keys are absent — even if a regression removes AR invoice/receipt generation entirely. Should assert keys exist first, then check the tie-out.

title: leases_eu.py (617 lines) has zero test coverage
description: generator/model/leases_eu.py contains IFRS 16 PV calculations (_pv_lease_payments line 135), IBR rate lookups (_ibr_eu_for_term line 126), amendment processing (lines 513-539), short-term/low-value exemption logic (lines 551-556), and computed properties. The end_date property (lines 103-108) has a subtle edge case when month==12. No test file imports it. tc04_eu formatter test does not exist — only test_eu_e2e.py touches TC-04-EU at a high level checking directory existence, not content.

title: scoring_template.py has zero test coverage (474 lines)
description: scoring/scoring_template.py generates a 4-sheet Excel workbook with formulas, conditional formatting, and Cohen's kappa calculation. No test verifies sheet structure, formula correctness, or valid xlsx output. The kappa formula (line 264) and AVERAGEIFS (line 327) are completely unverified. Only indirect coverage via baseline checksums which don't validate behavior.

title: Ghost directories TC-22..TC-30 created by _create_directory_tree()
description: When running with --packs all, _create_directory_tree() creates sequential directories TC-01 through TC-30 based on tc_count=30. However, the 9 EU variant test cases use TC-XX-EU naming (e.g. TC-04-EU, TC-06-EU), not TC-22 through TC-30. This leaves 9 empty ghost directories (TC-22/ through TC-30/) containing only empty input_files/ subdirs. Fix: Pass actual test case IDs to _create_directory_tree() instead of a count.

title: Relocate ERR-EU-018 to fixed assets Tax Rates sheet, add third lease, tighten goodwill headroom
description: TC-18-EU improvements: (1) Move ERR-EU-018 from revenue workpaper to fixed assets workpaper Tax Rates sheet (stale Munich Gewerbesteuer Hebesatz 480% vs 490%), making it more realistic and harder to detect. (2) Add a third new FY2025 lease (CM Lyon lab extension EU-LS-006). (3) Tighten CD CGU goodwill headroom from 6.8% to 3.5% to increase impairment risk signaling.
close_reason: All changes verified: 3597 tests pass, determinism check clean. ERR-EU-018 moved to fixed assets Tax Rates sheet, third lease added, goodwill headroom tightened.

title: Fix TC-12-EU data-room spreadsheet PDF rendering widths
description:
## Problem
Visual inspection of the generated all-pack suite at /tmp/synth-data-generated-docs-20260413-1 found that several TC-12-EU data-room workbooks render poorly when converted to PDF via LibreOffice. The rendered PDFs/images show numeric cells as ### and clipped headers/text, including group_budget_fy2025.xlsx, group_debt_schedule.xlsx, group_management_accounts_fy2025_ytd.xlsx, customer_list_with_revenue.xlsx, and vendor_list.xlsx. This appears to be a workbook column width / print layout problem rather than a generation failure: the source files open structurally, but the generated documents are not visually usable in PDF review.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Generate the all-pack suite: uv run python generate_test_suite.py --packs all --output /tmp/synth-data-generated-docs-20260413-1
2. Convert TC-12-EU data-room XLSX files to PDF with LibreOffice headless.
3. Render the first page of the converted PDFs with pdftoppm or open them visually.
4. Observe ### overflow markers and clipped headers/text in the affected TC-12-EU financial and operations workbooks.

## Expected
Affected workbooks render to PDF with readable numbers, complete headers, and no ### overflow markers.
acceptance_criteria: Regenerate TC-12-EU and confirm the affected workbooks render to PDF without ### overflow markers, clipped headers, or materially truncated cell text on the first rendered page. Add or update a document visual/render regression check that catches spreadsheet ### overflow in Office-to-PDF outputs.
notes: Classification review 2026-04-13: Treat as a test-case/generator-quality issue, not an evaluated-agent responsibility, unless we explicitly add a scenario requirement that agents must handle spreadsheet overflow markers. Current bead acceptance should remain fix-oriented: generated TC-12-EU XLSX/PDF renders should be visually usable and should not hide core diligence numbers behind ### overflow artifacts.
close_reason: Widened column widths by 25-40% across 6 TC-12-EU XLSX workbooks (management accounts, budget, debt schedule, customer list, vendor list, equipment list) and added landscape/fit-to-page setup via _set_landscape_fit() helper to prevent ### overflow in LibreOffice PDF rendering. Baseline checksums regenerated.

title: Document European variants and source-standard assumptions
description:
Update SPEC.md/specs and project docs for European variants, including pack selection, source-standard assumptions, jurisdiction scope, and case matrix.

The docs should link official research anchors and explain where Europe has no single unified analogue to US forms or state tax regimes.
acceptance_criteria: SPEC.md links the European variants spec; docs list affected TCs, source standards, jurisdiction choices, generation command, scoring differences, and non-goal caveats for legal/tax advice.
close_reason: Documentation complete: docs/european-variants.md (new), README.md updated with EU pack table and generation commands, docs/architecture.md updated with EU pack row, SPEC.md already linked EU specs. All acceptance criteria met.

title: Implement TC-18-EU IFRS/ISA rollforward variant
description: Implement the TC-18-EU IFRS/ISA rollforward variant with deterministic prior-year and current-year workpapers, gold, and rubric.
acceptance_criteria: Generated files open cleanly; canaries embedded; gold identifies successfully rolled-forward files and judgment-call files; rubric expects IFRS/ISA language; self-test passes; old TC-18 remains unchanged.
close_reason: Implemented TC-18-EU IFRS/ISA rollforward variant: 15 input files (6 xlsx workpapers + 4 docx memos + 5 current-year data), prompt.md, expected_behavior.md, gold standard with 7 judgment traps, ERR-EU-018 stale CP product revenue, Pillar Two threshold trap, multi-currency EUR+GBP, IAS 36 CGU-based impairment, IAS 16 revaluation model. 49 tests pass.

title: Design TC-18-EU IFRS/ISA rollforward variant
description:
Design a European variant of TC-18 prior-year workpaper rollforward using IFRS/ISA framing where US GAAP or US audit wording is load-bearing.

## Decision
Create TC-18-EU as an IFRS/ISA rollforward variant with prior/current-year workpapers, IFRS presentation language, ISA-style audit documentation expectations, and selected new-year judgment areas such as IAS 36 impairment or IFRS 16 lease updates.

## Rationale
Rollforward mechanics are general, but a European variant should test whether the agent can preserve structure while adapting language and judgment areas to IFRS/ISA framing.

## Alternatives Considered
1. Leave TC-18 unchanged. It tests rollforward, but not European reporting language.
2. Build a full audit methodology simulation. Too broad for a variant.
3. Add an IFRS/ISA-framed rollforward with a few selected judgment deltas. Preferred for v1.
design:
## TC-18-EU: IFRS/ISA Prior-Year Workpaper Rollforward

### Overview
European variant of TC-18 replacing the US GAAP/PCAOB-framed workpaper rollforward with an IFRS/ISA rollforward for Cascade Europe Holdings B.V. group. The US TC-18 assumes US GAAP chart of accounts terminology, PCAOB-style audit documentation, and US audit judgment areas (goodwill impairment under ASC 350). TC-18-EU replaces these with IFRS presentation language, ISA-style audit documentation expectations, and European judgment areas (IAS 36 impairment, IFRS 16 lease modifications, IAS 21 FX translation).

**Difficulty**: Adversarial (same as US TC-18)
**Entity scope**: Consolidated group (CE, CP, CM, CD)
**Reporting framework**: IFRS as adopted by EU
**Audit framework**: ISA (IAASB)

### Prior-Year Workpapers (FY2024) — `prior_year_workpapers/`

6 xlsx workpapers and 4 docx memos, reframed for IFRS/ISA:

**XLSX Workpapers (6)**:
1. **wp_revenue_fy2024.xlsx** — Revenue by entity and segment under IFRS 15 disaggregation. Includes intercompany elimination column for CP→CM, CP→CD, CE management fee revenue. Uses European number formatting convention notes (comma decimal separators in source ledgers).
2. **wp_operating_expenses_fy2024.xlsx** — Operating expenses by nature (IAS 1 classification by nature, not function as in US GAAP). Includes payroll costs with country-specific social charges (DE ~20%, FR ~45%, UK ~13.8%, NL ~20%).
3. **wp_balance_sheet_fy2024.xlsx** — Consolidated statement of financial position (IFRS terminology: "statement of financial position" not "balance sheet"). Right-of-use assets separated per IFRS 16, deferred tax split per IAS 12.
4. **wp_cash_fy2024.xlsx** — Statement of cash flows. Interest paid classified under financing activities (IAS 7 policy choice — different from typical US GAAP operating classification).
5. **wp_fixed_assets_fy2024.xlsx** — Property, plant and equipment roll-forward by entity. Component depreciation applied (IAS 16 requirement, less common under US GAAP). Includes revaluation reserve for CP manufacturing equipment (IAS 16 revaluation model — not permitted under US GAAP).
6. **wp_leases_fy2024.xlsx** — IFRS 16 lease schedules by entity. Single lessee model (no operating/finance distinction). Includes low-value asset exemptions and short-term exemptions. Incremental borrowing rates per entity based on EURIBOR/SONIA.

**DOCX Memos (4)**:
1. **memo_planning_fy2024.docx** — ISA 300 audit planning memorandum. References ISA 315 risk assessment, ISA 330 audit responses, ISA 600 group audit considerations (component auditors in DE, FR, UK). Uses ISA terminology: "significant risks," "component auditors," "group engagement team."
2. **memo_risk_assessment_fy2024.docx** — ISA 315 risk assessment. Identifies IFRS-specific risk areas: IFRS 16 lease modification judgments, IAS 36 impairment testing for goodwill allocated to CGUs, IAS 12 deferred tax on intercompany unrealized profits, IAS 21 FX translation of UK subsidiary (GBP→EUR).
3. **memo_summary_fy2024.docx** — Audit completion summary using ISA 220 quality management language. References Key Audit Matters (KAM) per ISA 701 (not Critical Audit Matters/CAM as under PCAOB). Summarizes component auditor communications per ISA 600.
4. **memo_management_letter_fy2024.docx** — Management letter to Cascade Europe Holdings B.V. board. References specific IFRS presentation and disclosure deficiencies. Includes recommendation on transfer pricing documentation (OECD master file completeness).

### Current-Year Data (FY2025) — `current_year_data/`

5 files with the same structural complexity as US TC-18:

1. **trial_balance_fy2025.csv** — Updated consolidated trial balance. Format change from xlsx (same trap as US TC-18). IFRS account descriptions (e.g., "Right-of-use assets" not "Operating lease assets," "Contract liabilities" not "Deferred revenue"). Amounts in EUR with GBP→EUR translation applied for CD.
2. **bank_statements_fy2025.csv** — Updated bank statements across 4 entities, 3 currencies (EUR for CE/CP/CM, GBP for CD). Multi-currency complicates reconciliation.
3. **lease_schedule_fy2025.xlsx** — Updated IFRS 16 lease schedule with 2 new leases added (same structure as US TC-18). New leases: one CP factory extension (EUR, EURIBOR-based IBR) and one CD warehouse (GBP, SONIA-based IBR). Both require right-of-use asset and lease liability calculation.
4. **management_projections_fy2025.docx** — Management's updated projections. Format change from xlsx (same trap as US TC-18). Now includes Pillar Two impact assessment section (even though group is below €750M threshold — judgment trap: agent should note threshold status rather than applying Pillar Two calculations).
5. **goodwill_impairment_analysis_ifrs.xlsx** — NEW file not in prior year (same structural role as US TC-18). IAS 36 impairment test for goodwill allocated to cash-generating units (CGUs), replacing the US ASC 350 single-step approach. Includes value-in-use calculations with discount rates per CGU and management's cash flow projections. Uses a pre-tax discount rate (IAS 36 requirement, vs. post-tax common in practice — subtle judgment area).

### Planted Error: ERR-EU-018

**Type**: stale_data
**Severity**: material
**Location**: wp_revenue_fy2024.xlsx, Revenue Summary sheet, CP (Germany) Product Revenue row

**Description**: The CP product revenue line shows the FY2023 value (€38.2M) instead of the FY2024 value (€40.1M). This mirrors the US TC-18 ERR-010 pattern (stale prior-year data in the revenue workpaper) but in the European IFRS context. The agent rolling forward this workpaper should notice the FY2023 figure doesn't match the FY2024 trial balance for CP product revenue and flag the stale data.

**Why this error works**: The workpaper header says "FY2024" and all other entity rows show correct FY2024 figures. Only the CP product revenue row carries forward FY2023 data, creating a subtle inconsistency that the agent must catch during the rollforward reconciliation step.

### Prompt

```
Roll forward the FY2024 IFRS audit workpapers to FY2025 using the current year
data for Cascade Europe Holdings B.V. group.

1. For each prior year workpaper, identify which data needs to be updated with
   FY2025 figures.
2. Map the current year data files to the corresponding prior year workpapers,
   noting any format changes (e.g., CSV vs. xlsx).
3. Update all numerical data with current year figures, ensuring IFRS
   presentation is preserved (statement of financial position, right-of-use
   assets, contract liabilities, etc.).
4. Preserve the workpaper structure and any ISA-referenced commentary that is
   still relevant.
5. Flag the following for the group engagement partner's attention:
   - Any structural changes in the client's data (new accounts, format changes,
     renamed fields)
   - The new IAS 36 goodwill impairment analysis file (not present in prior
     year — indicates a new significant risk area for the group audit)
   - The Pillar Two impact assessment in management projections (assess
     whether the group meets the €750M revenue threshold)
   - Any areas where prior year ISA 315 risk assessment commentary may no
     longer be applicable given current year developments
   - FX translation impacts from the UK subsidiary's GBP→EUR conversion
6. Update the ISA 300 planning memo with current year scope considerations,
   including any changes to component auditor arrangements.

Export the rolled-forward workpapers to a new folder.
```

### Gold Standard / Expected Behavior

The agent should successfully update 8 of 10 files (same ratio as US TC-18). The 2 requiring judgment flags:

1. **Management projections** — format change from xlsx to docx AND includes Pillar Two section that requires threshold assessment (group revenue ~€120M < €750M threshold → Pillar Two does not apply, but agent should note the assessment rather than silently ignoring it)
2. **ISA 300 planning memo** — needs substantive rewriting for FY2025 scope, not just data updates. Component auditor arrangements, updated ISA 315 risk assessment references, and new significant risk areas (IAS 36 goodwill impairment) require professional judgment.

**Key rollforward expectations**:
- CSV format change handled transparently (same as US TC-18)
- IFRS terminology preserved throughout (not converted to US GAAP language)
- ISA references maintained in memos (ISA 315, ISA 330, ISA 600, ISA 701)
- IAS 36 goodwill impairment file flagged as new significant audit area
- Pillar Two threshold correctly assessed as not applicable
- FX translation for CD (GBP→EUR) reflected in updated balance sheet and P&L workpapers
- ERR-EU-018 stale data in CP revenue workpaper detected or propagated (if propagated, agent loses robustness points)
- New leases (CP factory extension, CD warehouse) reflected in updated lease schedule
- Revaluation reserve for CP equipment carried forward correctly
- Interest classification under financing activities preserved (IAS 7 policy choice)

### Scoring Rubric Differences from US TC-18

| Dimension | US TC-18 | TC-18-EU |
|---|---|---|
| Accounting framework | US GAAP (ASC references) | IFRS (IAS/IFRS references) |
| Audit framework | PCAOB/US GAAS | ISA (IAASB) |
| Terminology | Balance sheet, operating lease, deferred revenue | Statement of financial position, right-of-use asset, contract liability |
| Impairment standard | ASC 350 (single-step goodwill) | IAS 36 (CGU-based, value-in-use with pre-tax discount rate) |
| Multi-entity complexity | Single entity (Cascade Industries) | 4-entity group with component auditors and intercompany eliminations |
| Currency complexity | USD only | EUR + GBP (FX translation for CD) |
| Lease framework | ASC 842 (operating vs. finance) | IFRS 16 (single model, no classification) |
| Additional judgment areas | Goodwill impairment (new file) | Goodwill impairment (new file) + Pillar Two threshold assessment + FX translation + component auditor scope |
| Planted error | ERR-010: stale FY2023 revenue data | ERR-EU-018: stale FY2023 CP product revenue data |
| Audit memo language | PCAOB AS 2110, AS 2201, CAM | ISA 315, ISA 330, ISA 600, ISA 701, KAM |
| P&L classification | By function (US GAAP typical) | By nature (IAS 1 policy choice) |
| Interest classification | Operating activities (US GAAP typical) | Financing activities (IAS 7 policy choice) |

### Judgment Traps

1. **US GAAP terminology substitution**: Agent rewrites IFRS terminology to US GAAP equivalents during rollforward (e.g., "balance sheet" instead of "statement of financial position," "operating lease" instead of IFRS 16 right-of-use asset).

2. **ISA→PCAOB reference swap**: Agent replaces ISA references in memos with PCAOB/AICPA references (e.g., "AS 2110" instead of "ISA 315," "CAM" instead of "KAM").

3. **Single-entity assumption**: Agent treats the rollforward as a single-entity exercise, ignoring intercompany eliminations, component auditor arrangements, and multi-currency translation.

4. **Pillar Two over-application**: Agent applies Pillar Two/GloBE calculations to the group despite revenue being below the €750M threshold. The management projections mention Pillar Two to test whether the agent assesses applicability before computing.

5. **Ignoring FX translation**: Agent rolls forward the UK subsidiary's numbers without applying GBP→EUR translation at updated rates, or uses a single exchange rate instead of distinguishing closing rate (balance sheet) vs. average rate (P&L) per IAS 21.

6. **Revaluation model ignorance**: Agent treats CP's revalued equipment as cost-model only, losing the revaluation reserve movement in the rollforward.

7. **Interest reclassification**: Agent moves interest paid back to operating activities (US GAAP default) during rollforward, overriding the IAS 7 policy choice to classify under financing.

### Input Files

1. **prior_year_workpapers/** — 10 files:
   - 6 xlsx: wp_revenue_fy2024.xlsx, wp_operating_expenses_fy2024.xlsx, wp_balance_sheet_fy2024.xlsx, wp_cash_fy2024.xlsx, wp_fixed_assets_fy2024.xlsx, wp_leases_fy2024.xlsx

Key differences from US TC-18: (1) IFRS terminology throughout — statement of financial position, right-of-use assets, contract liabilities, classification by nature (IAS 1); (2) ISA audit framework — ISA 315 risk assessment, ISA 600 component auditors, ISA 701 KAM instead of PCAOB/CAM; (3) Multi-entity group with 4 entities, intercompany eliminations, and component auditor arrangements; (4) Multi-currency: EUR + GBP with IAS 21 translation (closing rate for BS, average for P&L); (5) IAS 36 CGU-based impairment replaces ASC 350 single-step; (6) IAS 16 revaluation model for CP equipment (not permitted under US GAAP); (7) IAS 7 interest in financing activities (policy choice); (8) Pillar Two threshold trap (€120M < €750M → not applicable); (9) ERR-EU-018 mirrors ERR-010 — stale FY2023 CP product revenue data; (10) 7 judgment traps testing US-framing bias across terminology, audit standards, entity scope, Pillar Two, FX, revaluation, and interest classification. 15 canary keys with tc18eu_ prefix.

title: Design TC-16-EU European engagement letter variant
description:
Design a European variant of TC-16 engagement letter generation.

## Decision
Create TC-16-EU with European service scope and fee schedule assumptions, replacing US federal/state return fees with IFRS audit, corporate income tax, VAT compliance, transfer pricing support, and optional statutory accounts depending on the selected jurisdiction profile.

## Rationale
The current engagement letter includes US federal/state tax assumptions. A European variant should test template merge and professional scope language with European service lines and VAT/tax framing.

## Alternatives Considered
1. Only change address/currency. Rejected because US tax scope remains.
2. Create a jurisdiction-specific legal engagement letter. Too legal-specific for v1.
3. Add a European professional-services scope with IFRS/VAT/CIT fee drivers. Preferred for benchmark comparability.
design:
## TC-16-EU: European Engagement Letter Variant

### Overview
European variant of TC-16 replacing the US engagement letter generation with a European multi-service engagement letter for Cascade Europe Holdings B.V. and subsidiaries. The US TC-16 assumes US federal/state tax returns, IPO complexity multiplier, and USD fee schedule. TC-16-EU replaces these with European service lines (IFRS statutory audit, corporate income tax compliance across 4 jurisdictions, VAT compliance, transfer pricing support), EUR fee schedule, and European professional language and caveats.

**Difficulty**: Routine (same as US TC-16)
**Entity scope**: All four entities (CE, CP, CM, CD)
**Jurisdictions**: Netherlands, Germany, France, UK

### Client Profile Fields (EU)

Replace US client profile with European group profile:

| US TC-16 Field | EU Equivalent |
|---|---|
| Company Name: Cascade Industries, Inc. | Company Name: Cascade Europe Holdings B.V. |
| Entity Type: US C-Corporation | Entity Type: Dutch B.V. (Besloten Vennootschap) with 3 subsidiaries (GmbH, SAS, Ltd) |
| Headquarters: Portland, Oregon | Registered Office: Amsterdam, Netherlands |
| Revenue: ~$200M | Consolidated Revenue: ~€120M |
| Revenue Tier: $100M–$500M | Revenue Tier: €75M–€250M |
| Entities: 4 (1 parent + 3 subs) | Entities: 4 (CE + CP + CM + CD) |
| IPO Readiness: Yes | IFRS First-Time Adoption: Yes — group transitioning from Dutch GAAP to full IFRS |
| Multi-State: OR, TX, IL, CA, WA, NY | Multi-Jurisdiction: NL, DE, FR, UK |
| Fiscal Year End: December 31 | Fiscal Year End: 31 December |
| Key Contact CEO: Robert Chen | Key Contact Managing Director: Hans van der Berg |
| Key Contact CFO: Maria Santos | Key Contact Group Finance Director: Isabelle Moreau |
| Key Contact VP Finance: David Kim | Key Contact Group Controller: Klaus Fischer |
| External Counsel: Patterson & Associates LLP | External Counsel: De Brauw Blackstone Westbroek (NL) |

### Fee Schedule (EU)

Replace USD fee matrix with EUR fee matrix. Service lines change from US Audit/Tax Federal & State/Advisory to European equivalents:

**Service Lines**:
1. **Statutory Audit (IFRS)** — Consolidated IFRS audit of the group
2. **Corporate Income Tax (CIT) Compliance** — Per-entity CIT filings (NL Vpb, DE KSt, FR IS, UK CT)
3. **VAT Compliance** — Per-entity VAT return preparation and advisory
4. **Transfer Pricing Documentation** — Master file/local file per OECD BEPS Action 13

**Fee Matrix Columns**: Service Type | Revenue Tier | Entity Count Range | Base Fee (€) | Per-Entity Adder (€) | Complexity Multiplier

**Revenue Tiers (EUR)**:
- Under €25M
- €25M–€75M
- €75M–€250M (Cascade Europe matches here at ~€120M)
- Over €250M

**Fee data for Cascade Europe's tier (€75M–€250M, 4–6 entities)**:
- Statutory Audit (IFRS): Base €210,000, Per-Entity Adder €18,000, Complexity 1.00x
- CIT Compliance: Base €72,000, Per-Entity Adder €15,000, Complexity 1.00x
- VAT Compliance: Base €35,000, Per-Entity Adder €8,000, Complexity 1.00x
- Transfer Pricing: Base €48,000, Per-Entity Adder €12,000, Complexity 1.00x

**Complexity Multiplier Notes (EU)**:
- IFRS First-Time Adoption: 1.20x (applied to statutory audit when transitioning to IFRS)
- Cross-Border Restructuring: 1.15x (applied when group has active restructuring)
- Pillar Two Readiness: 1.10x (applied for GloBE top-up tax preparation)
- UK Post-Brexit Customs: 1.10x (applied to VAT compliance when UK entity has import VAT)

### Gold Standard Fee Calculation

The agent must identify Cascade Europe's revenue tier (€75M–€250M) and entity count (4 → "4–6" range) from the client profile.

1. **Statutory Audit**: Base €210,000 × 1.20 IFRS first-time adoption = **€252,000**
2. **CIT Compliance**: Base €72,000 + (€15,000 × 3 additional entities) = **€117,000**
3. **VAT Compliance**: Base €35,000 + (€8,000 × 3 additional entities) = **€59,000**
4. **Transfer Pricing**: Base €48,000 + (€12,000 × 3 additional entities) = **€84,000**
5. **Total Engagement Fee**: €252,000 + €117,000 + €59,000 + €84,000 = **€512,000**

### Planted Error: ERR-EU-016

**Type**: formula_error
**Severity**: material
**Location**: fee_schedule_eu.xlsx, Cascade Europe Fee Summary, Statutory Audit Total Fee cell

**Description**: The summary section shows the statutory audit total as €210,000 instead of €252,000 — the IFRS first-time adoption complexity multiplier (1.20x) was not applied to the base fee. This mirrors the US TC-16 ERR-013 pattern (IPO multiplier omission) but with the European IFRS adoption complexity driver.

**Why this error works**: The fee schedule correctly lists the 1.20x IFRS first-time adoption multiplier in the complexity notes, and the summary row even shows "IFRS First-Time Adoption 1.20x" in the Adjustments column, but the Total Fee cell uses the base fee without multiplication. The agent must detect the discrepancy between the stated adjustment and the computed total.

### Merge Fields

The engagement letter template uses the same <<field_name>> format. EU-specific merge field values:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| <<client_name>> | Cascade Europe Holdings B.V. |
| <<engagement_scope>> | Combined statutory audit (IFRS), corporate income tax compliance (NL/DE/FR/UK), VAT compliance, and transfer pricing documentation engagement, including IFRS first-time adoption procedures |
| <<fee_amount>> | €512,000 (or breakdown: Audit €252,000 + CIT €117,000 + VAT €59,000 + TP €84,000) |
| <<payment_terms>> | Net 30 |
| <<start_date>> | 15 March 2026 |
| <<partner_name>> | Pieter de Jong |

### Template

Use a European variant of the engagement letter template. Key differences from US:
- Firm name: "Mitchell & Partners International LLP" (European practice)
- Firm address: Zuidas, Amsterdam, Netherlands
- Template sections remain the same (Engagement Scope, Fees and Billing, Engagement Period, Responsibilities, Confidentiality, Limitation of Liability, Acceptance)
- Add EU-specific clauses in Engagement Scope:
  - "Statutory audits will be conducted in accordance with International Standards on Auditing (ISA) as adopted in the respective jurisdictions"
  - "Corporate income tax compliance covers annual CIT returns in all four jurisdictions (Netherlands, Germany, France, United Kingdom)"
  - "Transfer pricing documentation will follow OECD BEPS Action 13 requirements (Master File and Local File)"
  - "VAT compliance covers periodic VAT return preparation for all EU-registered and UK-registered entities"
- Limitation of Liability references Netherlands law (applicable to B.V. engagement)

### Prompt

```
Generate a draft engagement letter for a combined audit, tax, and advisory
engagement for Cascade Europe Holdings B.V. using the template provided.

1. Look up the appropriate fees from the fee schedule based on the client's
   revenue tier and entity count.
2. The statutory audit fee should include the complexity multiplier for IFRS
   first-time adoption (1.20x) since the group is transitioning to IFRS.
3. The CIT compliance fee should cover annual corporate income tax returns
   for the parent and 3 subsidiaries across all four jurisdictions.
4. The VAT compliance fee should cover all EU and UK VAT-registered entities.
5. The transfer pricing fee should cover OECD master file and local files.
6. Populate the template with the correct values.
7. Set the start date to 15 March 2026 and payment terms to Net 30.
8. Use "Pieter de Jong" as the engagement partner.

Save as a Word document.
```

### Expected Behavior

**Fee Calculation**:
- Identify revenue tier (€75M–€250M) and entity count (4 → 4–6 range)
- Statutory Audit: €210,000 × 1.20 = €252,000
- CIT Compliance: €72,000 + (€15,000 × 3) = €117,000
- VAT Compliance: €35,000 + (€8,000 × 3) = €59,000
- Transfer Pricing: €48,000 + (€12,000 × 3) = €84,000
- Total: €512,000

**Merge Field Population**:
- All 6 fields populated with EU-specific values
- Engagement scope describes IFRS audit + CIT + VAT + TP services

**Template Preservation**:
- All sections intact
- European firm name and address preserved
- ISA/IFRS/OECD language in scope section preserved

**ERR-EU-016 Detection**:
- Fee schedule summary shows €210,000 for audit instead of €252,000
- Agent should either flag the discrepancy or correctly use €252,000 despite the summary error

### Scoring Rubric Differences from US TC-16

| Dimension | US TC-16 | TC-16-EU |
|---|---|---|
| Fee complexity | 2 service lines (Audit + Tax) | 4 service lines (Audit + CIT + VAT + TP) |
| Complexity driver | IPO readiness (1.15x) | IFRS first-time adoption (1.20x) |
| Tax scope | Federal & state returns | Per-jurisdiction CIT filings (NL/DE/FR/UK) |
| Additional services | N/A | VAT compliance + transfer pricing documentation |
| Template language | US professional services | European/ISA/IFRS professional services |
| Currency | USD | EUR |
| Planted error | ERR-013: IPO multiplier omission | ERR-EU-016: IFRS adoption multiplier omission |
| Partner name | Sarah Chen | Pieter de Jong |

### Judgment Traps

1. **US fee framing**: Agent uses US revenue tiers or USD amounts when EUR fee schedule is provided.

2. **Single-jurisdiction assumption**: Agent calculates CIT/VAT for parent only, ignoring the 3 subsidiaries across 4 jurisdictions.

3. **Ignoring IFRS complexity**: Agent uses 1.00x multiplier for audit, missing the IFRS first-time adoption driver (mirroring the US IPO trap).

4. **Fee summary trust**: Agent copies the summary total from the fee schedule (which contains ERR-EU-016) instead of computing from base fee × multiplier.

5. **Template scope mismatch**: Agent populates engagement scope with US-style language (federal/state returns, GAAP) instead of European terminology (ISA, IFRS, CIT, VAT, OECD TP).

### Input Files

1. **client_profile_eu.docx** — European group profile with entity structure, revenue, IFRS transition status, jurisdictions, key contacts
2. **fee_schedule_eu.xlsx** — EUR fee matrix with 4 service lines, EU revenue tiers, complexity multipliers, and Cascade Europe fee summary (containing ERR-EU-016)
3. **engagement_letter_template_eu.docx** — European variant template with ISA/IFRS language and Netherlands law limitation of liability

### Canary Prefix
All generated files use `tc16eu_` prefix for canary keys.

### File Count
3 input files + 1 prompt.md + 1 expected_behavior.md = 5 files (same count as US TC-16).
acceptance_criteria: Design defines client profile fields, fee schedule, template clauses, scope wording, VAT/tax compliance services, professional language caveats, prompt, gold expectations, and rubric differences from the US engagement letter.
close_reason: Design complete. TC-16-EU replaces US engagement letter with European multi-service variant for Cascade Europe Holdings B.V. Key changes from US TC-16: (1) 4 service lines (IFRS Statutory Audit, CIT Compliance NL/DE/FR/UK, VAT Compliance, Transfer Pricing) instead of 2 (Audit, Tax Federal & State); (2) EUR fee schedule with €75M-€250M revenue tiers; (3) IFRS first-time adoption complexity multiplier 1.20x replaces IPO readiness 1.15x; (4) Total fee €512,000 (Audit €252K + CIT €117K + VAT €59K + TP €84K); (5) ERR-EU-016 mirrors ERR-013 pattern — IFRS adoption multiplier omission in summary (shows €210K instead of €252K); (6) European template with ISA/IFRS/OECD language and NL law limitation of liability; (7) 4 judgment traps testing US-framing bias, single-jurisdiction assumption, IFRS complexity ignorance, and fee summary trust. Canary prefix tc16eu_.

title: Design TC-12-EU European diligence data room variant
description:
Design a European variant of TC-12 data room triage that replaces US corporate/tax documents with European equivalents where US documents are load-bearing.

## Decision
Create TC-12-EU as a European diligence data room variant using the selected jurisdiction profile, European company constitutional documents, IFRS financials, VAT/CIT filings or summaries, contracts, employment/HR documents where selected, and source-backed red flags.

## Rationale
TC-12 already tests data-room triage, but its corporate and tax document inventory is US-heavy. A European variant should preserve the diligence skill while changing the document corpus and red flags.

## Alternatives Considered
1. Keep the same TC-12 documents. Rejected because US articles/federal returns remain load-bearing.
2. Replace every data-room item. Unnecessary and risks losing comparability.
3. Map US-specific classes to European equivalents while keeping generic diligence categories. Preferred for v1.
design:
## TC-12-EU: European Diligence Data Room Variant

### Overview
European variant of TC-12 replacing the US-focused corporate acquisition data room with a European data room for the potential acquisition of Cascade Europe Holdings B.V. and its subsidiaries. The US TC-12 assumes articles of incorporation, bylaws, federal/state tax returns, and US employment agreements — all of which are load-bearing for triage and red flag identification. TC-12-EU replaces these with jurisdiction-specific European equivalents (Dutch KvK extracts, German Handelsregister excerpts, French K-bis, UK Companies House filings, IFRS financials, VAT/CIT filings, works council documentation) while preserving the same core diligence skill: index, cross-reference, flag, and prioritize.

**Difficulty**: Adversarial (same as US TC-12)
**Entity scope**: All four entities (CE, CP, CM, CD)
**Jurisdictions**: Netherlands, Germany, France, UK

### Document Class Mapping (US → EU)

| US TC-12 Category | US Documents | EU Equivalent | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| **01_corporate** | articles_of_incorporation.pdf | ce_kvk_extract.pdf (NL Chamber of Commerce) | NL B.V. has no articles in US sense; KvK extract is the public registration equivalent |
| | bylaws.pdf | ce_articles_of_association.pdf (NL statuten) | Dutch statuten are the internal governance equivalent of US bylaws |
| | board_minutes_2024.pdf | ce_board_minutes_2024.pdf | Generic — kept as-is |
| | board_minutes_2025.pdf | ce_board_minutes_2025.pdf | Generic — kept as-is |
| | org_chart.pdf | group_org_chart.pdf | Updated to show EU group structure with legal forms |
| **02_financial** | audited_financials_fy2023.pdf | group_ifrs_financials_fy2023.pdf | IFRS consolidated financials replace US GAAP |
| | audited_financials_fy2024.pdf | group_ifrs_financials_fy2024.pdf | IFRS consolidated financials |
| | management_financials_fy2025_ytd.xlsx | group_management_accounts_fy2025_ytd.xlsx | Same purpose, IFRS-framed |
| | budget_fy2025.xlsx | group_budget_fy2025.xlsx | Generic — kept as-is with EUR amounts |
| | debt_schedule.xlsx | group_debt_schedule.xlsx | Includes intercompany loan CE→CM |
| **03_legal** | customer_agreement_acme.pdf | customer_agreement_autohaus_mueller.pdf (DE) | German customer contract with Cascade Präzisionsteile |
| | customer_agreement_globex.pdf | customer_agreement_renault_tier2.pdf (FR) | French customer contract with Cascade Matériaux |
| | supplier_agreement_initech.pdf | supplier_agreement_thyssenkrupp_materials.pdf (DE) | German supplier agreement |
| | pending_litigation_summary.docx | pending_litigation_summary.docx | Adapted to EU jurisdictions (German labor court, French commercial court) |
| | ip_assignment_agreements.pdf | ip_assignment_agreements.pdf | Adapted: French-law IP assignments from CM researchers |
| | insurance_policies_summary.pdf | insurance_policies_summary.pdf | Generic — updated to EU insurers/coverages |
| **04_hr** | employee_census.xlsx | group_employee_census.xlsx | Multi-jurisdiction with country-specific fields (social insurance IDs, works council flags) |
| | benefits_summary.pdf | benefits_summary_by_country.pdf | Country-specific: German Betriebliche Altersvorsorge, French mutuelle, UK pension auto-enrollment |
| | ceo_employment_agreement.pdf | managing_director_service_agreement_ce.pdf | NL managing director (bestuurder) service agreement |
| | cfo_employment_agreement.pdf | finance_director_agreement_ce.pdf | |
| | cto_employment_agreement.pdf | technical_director_agreement_cm.pdf | Based in France (CM is the R&D entity) |
| | org_chart_detailed.xlsx | group_org_chart_detailed.xlsx | Multi-entity with reporting lines across borders |
| **05_tax** | federal_returns_fy2023.pdf | cp_korperschaftsteuer_fy2023.pdf (DE CIT return) | No US federal equivalent — each entity files locally |
| | federal_returns_fy2024.pdf | cp_korperschaftsteuer_fy2024.pdf (DE CIT return) | |
| | state_returns_summary.xlsx | group_vat_returns_summary.xlsx | VAT returns across NL/DE/FR/UK replace US state returns |
| | tax_notices.pdf | tax_notices_and_assessments.pdf | Includes a German Betriebsprüfung (tax audit) notice |
| **05_tax (new)** | — | cm_liasse_fiscale_fy2024.pdf (FR tax package) | French CIT filing — no US equivalent, adds cross-border complexity |
| | — | cd_ct600_fy2024.pdf (UK corporation tax return) | UK CIT filing |
| | — | ce_vpb_aangifte_fy2024.pdf (NL CIT return) | Dutch CIT filing |
| **06_operations** | facility_leases.pdf | facility_leases.pdf | Updated to EU locations (Munich, Lyon, Birmingham, Amsterdam) |
| | equipment_list.xlsx | equipment_list.xlsx | Generic — kept as-is with EUR amounts |
| | customer_list_with_revenue.xlsx | customer_list_with_revenue.xlsx | Multi-currency (EUR/GBP), multi-entity |
| | vendor_list.xlsx | vendor_list.xlsx | Multi-jurisdiction vendors |
| **07_technology** | patent_portfolio.pdf | patent_portfolio.pdf | Updated to EPO (European Patent Office) patents and national patents |
| | software_licenses.xlsx | software_licenses.xlsx | Generic — kept as-is |
| | it_infrastructure_overview.docx | it_infrastructure_overview.docx | Updated to reflect EU data centers, GDPR compliance |
| **08_compliance (new)** | — | gdpr_data_processing_register.xlsx | EU-specific: no US equivalent. Article 30 GDPR register. |
| | — | works_council_agreements_cp.pdf (DE) | German Betriebsrat agreements — EU-specific labor governance |

### Data Room Structure (36 files total)

```
data_room/
├── 01_corporate/
│   ├── ce_kvk_extract.pdf
│   ├── ce_articles_of_association.pdf
│   ├── cp_handelsregister_auszug.pdf
│   ├── cm_kbis_extract.pdf
│   ├── cd_companies_house_filing.pdf
│   ├── ce_board_minutes_2024.pdf
│   ├── ce_board_minutes_2025.pdf
│   └── group_org_chart.pdf
├── 02_financial/
│   ├── group_ifrs_financials_fy2023.pdf
│   ├── group_ifrs_financials_fy2024.pdf
│   ├── group_management_accounts_fy2025_ytd.xlsx
│   ├── group_budget_fy2025.xlsx
│   └── group_debt_schedule.xlsx
├── 03_legal/
│   ├── material_contracts/
│   │   ├── customer_agreement_autohaus_mueller.pdf
│   │   ├── customer_agreement_renault_tier2.pdf
│   │   └── supplier_agreement_thyssenkrupp_materials.pdf
│   ├── pending_litigation_summary.docx
│   ├── ip_assignment_agreements.pdf
│   └── insurance_policies_summary.pdf
├── 04_hr/
│   ├── group_employee_census.xlsx
│   ├── benefits_summary_by_country.pdf
│   ├── key_employee_agreements/
│   │   ├── managing_director_service_agreement_ce.pdf
│   │   ├── finance_director_agreement_ce.pdf
│   │   └── technical_director_agreement_cm.pdf
│   └── group_org_chart_detailed.xlsx
├── 05_tax/
│   ├── cp_korperschaftsteuer_fy2023.pdf
│   ├── cp_korperschaftsteuer_fy2024.pdf
│   ├── cm_liasse_fiscale_fy2024.pdf
│   ├── cd_ct600_fy2024.pdf
│   ├── ce_vpb_aangifte_fy2024.pdf
│   ├── group_vat_returns_summary.xlsx
│   └── tax_notices_and_assessments.pdf
├── 06_operations/
│   ├── facility_leases.pdf
│   ├── equipment_list.xlsx
│   ├── customer_list_with_revenue.xlsx
│   └── vendor_list.xlsx
├── 07_technology/
│   ├── patent_portfolio.pdf
│   ├── software_licenses.xlsx
│   └── it_infrastructure_overview.docx
└── 08_compliance/
    ├── gdpr_data_processing_register.xlsx
    └── works_council_agreements_cp.pdf
```

Also provide:
- `dd_checklist_european.docx`: A European due diligence checklist with 72 line items organized by category (Corporate/Constitutional, Financial/IFRS, Tax/VAT, Legal, Employment/Labor, Operations, Technology/IP, Environmental, Regulatory/Compliance, Data Protection/GDPR). Expanded from 65 to 72 to cover EU-specific categories (GDPR, works councils, VAT registrations, transfer pricing documentation).

### Red Flags (pre-defined, known to gold standard)

1. **Pending litigation (German labor court)**: Former CP employee filed unfair dismissal claim at Munich Arbeitsgericht. Potential exposure €180K (German labor courts tend toward reinstatement or high severance). Distinct from US-style litigation because German labor law strongly favors employees.

2. **Managing director change-of-control clause**: CE managing director service agreement includes a 2.5× annual compensation severance triggered by change of control. Less extreme than US 3× golden parachute but still material for a B.V. acquisition.

3. **Customer agreement with change-of-control termination**: Renault Tier 2 supply agreement (CM) has a clause permitting termination on change of indirect control. This is the EU equivalent of the US Acme red flag.

4. **Missing GDPR data processing agreements**: The GDPR data processing register references 4 third-party processors but only 2 have executed Data Processing Agreements (DPAs) on file. Missing DPAs for the cloud hosting provider and the external HR/payroll vendor — a material GDPR compliance gap post-acquisition.

5. **IP assignment gap (French law)**: IP assignment agreements show assignments from 6 of 8 CM researchers. Under French law (Code de la propriété intellectuelle), employee inventions have specific assignment requirements (déclaration d'invention). Two founding researchers' assignments are missing — mirroring the US TC-12 founding employee gap but with French IP law implications.

6. **Expiring EPO patents**: Patent portfolio includes 2 European patents (EP) expiring within 18 months. One covers a core manufacturing process used by CP.

7. **German tax audit (Betriebsprüfung) notice**: Tax notices file includes an open Betriebsprüfung for CP covering FY2022-2023. German tax audits can result in reassessments with interest. The audit is still in progress — no final assessment yet. This is distinct from US tax notices because Betriebsprüfungen are routine but can produce significant adjustments.

8. **Works council consultation requirement**: CP (Germany, 310 employees) has a Betriebsrat. Under German law (Betriebsverfassungsgesetz), a change of control triggers works council consultation and information rights (§111-113 BetrVG). The works council agreement on file is from 2019 and may not cover acquisition scenarios. The agent should flag this as a deal process requirement.

9. **Missing documents (gaps)**:
   - Environmental impact assessments for CP Munich facility (German Immissionsschutz)
   - Transfer pricing documentation (Master File/Local File per OECD/EU TPD)
   - UK post-Brexit customs/trade compliance documentation for CD
   - French comité social et économique (CSE) consultation records for CM
   - Insurance claim history across all entities
   - Real property surveys for owned/leased facilities

### Prompt

```
You have access to a deal data room for the potential acquisition of Cascade Europe Holdings B.V. and its European subsidiaries (Germany, France, UK).

1. Create a complete document index: for each file, provide:
   - File path
   - Document type/category
   - Jurisdiction (NL, DE, FR, UK, or Group)
   - Date (if identifiable)
   - One-line summary of contents
   - Key data points or red flags noted

2. Cross-reference against the European due diligence checklist:
   - Mark which checklist items have corresponding documents
   - Flag checklist items with NO corresponding document (gaps)
   - Note any documents in the data room not covered by the checklist

3. Identify red flags or items requiring immediate attention, such as:
   - Pending litigation or regulatory proceedings
   - Contracts with change-of-control provisions
   - Key employee agreements with unusual terms
   - Missing critical documents
   - GDPR compliance gaps
   - Works council / employee representative body consultation requirements
   - Open tax audits or assessments

4. Prioritize the gaps: which missing documents are deal-critical vs. nice-to-have?
   Consider jurisdiction-specific requirements (e.g., German works council consultation is a legal requirement, not optional).

Export:
- Document index as Excel (with columns: Path, Category, Jurisdiction, Date, Summary, Red Flags)
- Gap analysis as Excel (Checklist Item, Status, Jurisdiction, Priority, Notes)
- Red flags summary as Word memo
```

### Gold Standard Expectations

The gold standard defines what a correct agent response must contain:

**Document Index (XLSX)**:
- All 36 data room files indexed with correct jurisdiction tags
- Correct categorization of jurisdiction-specific document types (e.g., KvK extract → Corporate/Registration, Liasse Fiscale → Tax/CIT)
- Red flag column populated for files containing embedded red flags

**Gap Analysis (XLSX)**:
- All 72 checklist items mapped to present/absent status
- Transfer pricing documentation flagged as missing (deal-critical)
- Environmental assessments flagged as missing (CP facility)
- GDPR DPAs partially present (2 of 4) — flagged as compliance gap
- CSE records for CM flagged as missing (French labor law requirement)
- UK customs compliance documentation flagged as missing (post-Brexit)

**Red Flags Memo (DOCX)**:
Must identify and correctly characterize:
1. Munich labor court claim (€180K exposure, note German labor law context)
2. Managing director change-of-control severance (2.5× compensation)
3. Renault contract change-of-control termination clause
4. Missing GDPR DPAs (2 of 4 processors)
5. IP assignment gaps (2 French researchers, note French IP law)
6. Expiring EPO patents (2 within 18 months)
7. Open Betriebsprüfung for CP (FY2022-2023)
8. Works council consultation requirement under §111-113 BetrVG

Priority ranking of missing items:
- **Deal-critical**: Transfer pricing documentation, works council consultation confirmation, GDPR DPAs, CSE records
- **High priority**: Environmental assessments (CP), UK customs compliance, insurance claim history
- **Nice-to-have**: Real property surveys, historical board minutes beyond 2024

### Judgment Traps

1. **US-framing trap**: Agent treats KvK extract as "articles of incorporation" or tries to find "bylaws" — shows it is applying US corporate document taxonomy to European entities.

2. **GDPR underweight**: Agent identifies missing DPAs but does not flag them as deal-critical. Post-GDPR, missing processor agreements are a material liability for the acquirer (potential fines up to 4% of global turnover).

3. **Works council omission**: Agent fails to flag the Betriebsrat consultation requirement. Under German law, this is not optional — it is a mandatory step in the acquisition process that affects deal timeline.

4. **Betriebsprüfung dismissal**: Agent treats the open German tax audit as routine and non-material. While Betriebsprüfungen are common, an open audit covering 2 years with no final assessment represents an unquantified tax liability.

5. **French IP law ignorance**: Agent flags the missing IP assignments but does not note the French-specific legal framework. Under French law, employee invention assignment is governed by specific statutory rules, not just contract clauses.

6. **UK post-Brexit blind spot**: Agent does not flag the absence of customs/trade compliance documentation for CD, or treats CD as an EU entity for VAT/customs purposes.

### Scoring Rubric Differences from US TC-12

| Dimension | US TC-12 | TC-12-EU |
|---|---|---|
| Document identification | US corporate documents | Jurisdiction-specific constitutional documents (KvK, Handelsregister, K-bis, Companies House) |
| Tax analysis | Federal/state returns | Per-entity CIT filings + VAT + Betriebsprüfung |
| Employment/labor | Individual agreements | Individual agreements + works council + CSE |
| Compliance | General | GDPR-specific (DPAs, processing register) |
| Cross-border | N/A (single jurisdiction) | 4 jurisdictions with different legal systems |
| Red flag characterization | US legal framework | Jurisdiction-specific legal frameworks (German labor, French IP, EU GDPR) |
| Gap prioritization | US regulatory context | Jurisdiction-specific mandatory requirements vs. nice-to-have |

### Planted Error

**ERR-EU-012**: In the group_vat_returns_summary.xlsx file (also used in TC-10-EU), the German entity CP's Q3 2025 output VAT amount is €847,200 but should be €874,200 (transposed digits). This is discoverable only by cross-referencing the intercompany sales data against the VAT return — testing whether the agent performs data integrity checks across data room documents rather than just indexing them.

### Canary Prefix
All generated files use `tc12eu_` prefix for canary keys, following the convention established in synth-data-eu.1.

### File Count
36 data room files + 1 checklist = 37 input files total (US TC-12: 32 + 1 = 33).
acceptance_criteria: Design maps each US TC-12 input class to an EU/European equivalent or explicitly keeps it generic; defines red flags, missing documents, source evidence, prompt, gold expectations, and rubric differences.
close_reason: Design complete. TC-12-EU replaces US corporate data room with European multi-jurisdiction equivalent for acquisition of Cascade Europe Holdings B.V. Key changes from US TC-12: (1) Corporate docs replaced with jurisdiction-specific filings (KvK, Handelsregister, K-bis, Companies House) across NL/DE/FR/UK; (2) Tax section expanded from federal/state to per-entity CIT returns + VAT summary + open Betriebsprüfung; (3) New 08_compliance category for GDPR register and works council agreements; (4) 9 red flags including German labor court claim, managing director CoC clause, Renault CoC termination, missing GDPR DPAs, French IP assignment gaps, expiring EPO patents, open tax audit, works council consultation requirement, and 6 missing document categories; (5) 6 judgment traps testing US-framing bias, GDPR underweighting, works council omission, tax audit dismissal, French IP law ignorance, UK post-Brexit blind spot; (6) Planted error ERR-EU-012 (transposed digits in CP Q3 VAT). 36 data room files + 1 European DD checklist (72 items, up from 65). Canary prefix tc12eu_.
