title: Implement TC-21 combined diligence findings formatter and prompt
description:
Add TC-21 combined mini data room and prompt for a client-ready legal/HR diligence findings memo.

Inputs should combine curated legal and HR artifacts with a management Q&A summary and diligence request tracker. The prompt should require executive summary, legal findings, HR findings, unresolved requests, assumptions/limitations, and citations.
acceptance_criteria: Formatter emits curated combined inputs; prompt requires findings, unresolved requests, assumptions/limitations, and citations; canaries are embedded; all inputs use supported file formats.
close_reason: Implemented TC-21 combined diligence findings formatter: curated mini data room (3 contracts, 2 amendments, 2 agreements, severance/retention schedules), management Q&A summary with embedded traps, combined legal+HR request tracker, prompt requiring 6 sections, expected behavior, gold standard with 6 evidence expectations and 5 judgment traps.

title: Update rubrics for evidence-backed professional judgment
description: Update scoring/rubrics.yaml and scoring docs to explain evidence expectations and professional judgment scoring for TC-19 through TC-21.
acceptance_criteria: Rubric contains concrete anchors for legal/HR diligence cases; docs explain primary-source reliance, missing evidence, assumptions, and caveated conclusions.
close_reason: Added evidence dimension to rubrics.yaml (generic anchors + per-TC anchors for TC-19/20/21). Expanded docs/scoring.md with detailed explanation of evidence scoring concepts: primary-source reliance, missing evidence, assumptions/limitations, caveated conclusions, scoring mechanics, and how judgment traps affect the standard dimensions. All 1070 tests pass.

title: Add evidence expectation checks to auto grader
description:
Add a lightweight evidence check that searches agent outputs for required source references and key terms from gold evidence_expectations.

This should augment the current mechanical grading without breaking old golds that do not define evidence expectations.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover full credit for primary-source citation, partial or failed evidence checks, and no regression for old golds without evidence_expectations; aggregate reports include the evidence result when present.
close_reason: Added check_evidence() to TestCaseGrader (searches agent output for required_sources and acceptable_terms) and SelfTestGrader (validates spec structure). Evidence dimension included in grade report only when evidence_expectations is present; backward-compatible for old golds. Aggregate reports include evidence stats. 11 new tests covering full credit, partial/fail, case insensitivity, backward compat, self-test, and aggregate inclusion. All 1064 tests pass.

title: Add CLI support for selecting scenario packs
description: Add --pack or --packs support to generate_test_suite.py so non-default scenario packs can be generated intentionally without changing default output.
acceptance_criteria: Default command emits accounting core only; explicit legal/HR pack command emits TC-19 through TC-21 after pack implementation; unknown pack exits with a clear error; help text documents available pack behavior.
close_reason: Implemented: Added --packs CLI argument to generate_test_suite.py. Omitting --packs generates default (accounting-core only); --packs all generates every registered pack; --packs <name> selects specific packs; unknown packs exit with a clear error. Help text documents available packs. 5 new tests in test_orchestrator.py.

title: Add optional scenario_pack metadata to manifest entries
description: Extend ManifestEntry and manifest JSON output with optional scenario_pack metadata while preserving compatibility for existing manifest consumers.
acceptance_criteria: Existing manifest tests pass after expected-schema updates; new tests assert scenario_pack metadata is stable, optional, and sorted deterministically; accounting-core entries can be associated with cascade_accounting_core without changing paths.
close_reason: Implemented: ManifestEntry has optional scenario_pack field. Manifest.set_current_pack() provides automatic tagging per-pack. Orchestrator sets pack context before running emitters. Field omitted from JSON when empty (backward-compatible). 6 new tests covering presence/absence, context, explicit override, determinism, and orchestrator integration.

title: Add canary-preservation tests for controlled document noise
description:
Add regression/property tests proving controlled document noise cannot erase, corrupt, or make canaries unfindable across the supported formatter matrix.

This closes the risk where a narrow pilot passes while another output family loses benchmark evidence during presentation-noise transforms.
acceptance_criteria: Tests run against the formatter-family matrix; canaries remain findable after supported noise transforms; failures identify TC/file/transform; deterministic generation and existing canary verification gates still pass.
close_reason: Implemented tests/test_canary_noise_preservation.py with 370 parametrized tests covering canary preservation across all 3 implemented noise families (xlsx_openpyxl, csv_stdlib, docx_python_docx). Tests verify: canary survives noise across 50 seeds per representative file, canary byte-identical after transforms, canary preserved with exclusion zones active, BOM tolerance for CSV canaries, and noise RNG isolation from canary registry. Each failure identifies TC/file_key/seed. All existing tests still pass.

title: Add augmentation feature gate and offline fallback contract
description:
Protect default generation from optional LLM/DataDesigner augmentation.

The implementation contract should ensure provider unavailability, missing credentials, cache misses, or disabled augmentation cannot affect canonical deterministic generation.
acceptance_criteria: Augmentation is off by default; default generation performs no provider calls; missing provider credentials produce a clear disabled/skip path; cache-miss behavior is explicit; tests prove canonical outputs remain byte-identical with augmentation disabled.
close_reason: Implemented augmentation feature gate: AugmentationConfig (disabled by default) in config.py, AugmentationCache class in generator/augmentation.py with no-op disabled path, augmentation_source field on ManifestEntry, 22 tests covering disabled/enabled paths, cache key determinism, config parsing, and byte-identical output verification.

title: Define augmentation baseline, acceptance metrics, and opt-in rollout gate
description:
Before investing further in DataDesigner-style augmentation, define the baseline comparison, fidelity metrics, and go/no-go rule.

This decision should keep augmentation framed as optional narrative/soft-fidelity enrichment, not a source of canonical accounting facts.

## Decision
Define the acceptance metrics and opt-in rollout gate before running or integrating further augmentation work. Augmentation remains optional and cannot become part of the default deterministic canonical generation path unless the gate is explicitly changed by a later decision.

## Rationale
The spike, cache design, and validators can all be locally useful while still failing to answer whether augmentation earns its complexity. A go/no-go gate keeps research bounded and protects the accounting-fact model from provider-driven nondeterminism.

## Alternatives Considered
1. Run the spike first and decide later. This may produce concrete examples but risks unbounded exploration without adoption criteria.
2. Adopt DataDesigner directly as a fidelity layer. This could improve narrative realism but would add provider, cache, and determinism risk too early.
3. Define metrics and opt-in criteria first. This is the preferred path because it makes the spike evaluable and keeps default generation provider-free.
design:
## Augmentation Baseline, Acceptance Metrics, and Opt-In Rollout Gate

### 1. Baseline Outputs (what we compare against)

The baseline is the current deterministic generator output at SEED=42:
- All files in `test_cases/TC-01..TC-18/input_files/`
- All files in `shared_data/`
- Gold standards in `gold_standards/`

Specifically, augmentation targets **soft-fidelity narrative text** — memo prose, engagement letter language, management discussion phrasing, workpaper commentary — NOT canonical accounting figures. The baseline for comparison is the template-generated text that currently fills these fields.

### 2. Fidelity Metrics

| Metric | Measurement | Threshold |
|--------|-------------|-----------|
| **Narrative realism** | Blind rating by 2 reviewers (1–5 Likert) on 10 sampled documents: "Does this read like a real Big 4 document?" | Mean ≥ 3.5 for augmented vs ≤ 3.0 for baseline |
| **Accounting-fact preservation** | Auto-grader self-test (all 18 TCs score 3/3/3/3/3 on correctness) after augmentation applied | 100% pass — zero regression |
| **Canary preservation** | `canary_registry.json` round-trip: every canary still findable in augmented output | 100% — no canary loss |
| **Planted-error preservation** | `error_registry.json` round-trip: every planted error still present and detectable | 100% — no error masked |
| **Determinism** | Two runs of generator with augmentation cache → `diff -r` empty | Byte-identical outputs |
| **Cache hit rate** | Fraction of augmentation requests served from cache vs live provider | ≥ 99% after initial warm-up (first-run cache miss is acceptable) |

### 3. Adoption Threshold (Go)

ALL of the following must hold:
- Accounting-fact, canary, and planted-error preservation: 100%
- Determinism: byte-identical across reruns (via cached artifacts)
- Narrative realism delta: augmented mean ≥ 0.5 Likert points above baseline on blind review
- No new external dependency at runtime when cache is warm (provider only needed for cache warm-up)
- `auto_grader.py --self-test` passes with zero regressions

### 4. Rejection Criteria (No-Go)

ANY of the following triggers rejection:
- Any canonical accounting figure altered by augmentation
- Any canary value lost or corrupted
- Any planted error masked or relocated
- Nondeterminism in outputs when cache is warm
- Narrative realism delta < 0.3 Likert points (not worth the complexity)
- Cache warm-up requires >$50 or >2 hours of provider API time

### 5. Required Validation

Before declaring go/no-go:
1. Run spike (synth-data-8ok.7.1) on one canonical seed dataset
2. Apply augmented artifacts to full 18-TC suite
3. Run `auto_grader.py --self-test` — must be all-green
4. Run determinism smoke test (generate twice, diff)
5. Conduct blind narrative review (2 reviewers, 10 documents)
6. Measure cache hit rate over 3 consecutive runs

### 6. Opt-In Rollout Gate

- Augmentation is OFF by default in `config.yaml` (`augmentation.enabled: false`)
- Enabling requires explicit `--augment` CLI flag or `augmentation.enabled: true` in config
- When disabled, the generator produces identical output to pre-augmentation baseline
- When enabled but cache is cold, generator falls back to baseline text (no live provider calls during generation)
- Cache warm-up is a separate explicit step: `python generate_test_suite.py --warm-augmentation-cache`

### 7. Default Generation Guarantee

The default generation path (`python generate_test_suite.py --output <dir>`) MUST remain:
- Deterministic (SEED=42, byte-identical)
- Provider-free (no network calls)
- Unchanged in output unless augmentation is explicitly opted into

This guarantee is non-negotiable and overrides any augmentation convenience.
acceptance_criteria: Decision defines baseline outputs, fidelity metrics, adoption threshold, rejection criteria, required validation, opt-in rollout gate, and confirmation that default generation remains deterministic and provider-free.
close_reason: Decision recorded with concrete metrics: baseline is current SEED=42 output; fidelity measured via narrative realism Likert (≥3.5), 100% accounting-fact/canary/error preservation, byte-identical determinism with cache; go requires ≥0.5 Likert delta and zero regressions; no-go on any canonical figure alteration or <0.3 delta; opt-in via config flag with offline fallback; default generation remains deterministic and provider-free.

title: Add TUI override round-trip and unsupported-field regression tests
description:
Add tests around the TUI/draft-service save path to prove override YAML saves are non-destructive and reload into the same effective configuration.

The tests should also cover unsupported-field handling so the UI cannot silently preserve, drop, or reinterpret unsupported configuration in a way that changes scenario semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover load, edit, save, reload, and merged diff equivalence; unsupported fields produce the documented validation behavior; atomic save does not mutate config.yaml; saved YAML reloads through the shared layered config loader.
close_reason: Added 24 regression tests in tests/test_override_roundtrip.py covering: override round-trip (8 tests), unsupported-field validation (8 tests), atomic save non-mutation (3 tests), and deep merge edge cases (5 tests). Tests define the contract for the layered config loader and draft service.

title: Define and test config compatibility and migration path
description:
Define how existing config.yaml files, new layered override files, and future config versions remain compatible.

This is the cross-cutting guardrail for preserving the existing --config workflow while adding presets, layered overrides, TUI saves, and scenario-specific validation.
design: See docs/plans/config-compatibility-v1.md for the full policy. Summary: (1) Strict unknown-field rejection at all levels (top, company, subsidiary) via _reject_unknown_keys in generator/config.py. (2) No config_version field in v1 — configs without it are v1. v2 will add config_version: 2 as a superset. (3) Existing config.yaml is the backward-compat contract — loads unchanged. (4) Build-time fields (canary_assignments, error_injections) accepted but not user-editable. (5) 10 new tests in tests/test_config.py covering unknown-field rejection, legacy compat, no-mutation, and version-less loading.
acceptance_criteria: Compatibility policy covers existing config.yaml, unknown or unsupported fields, versioning/migration stance, CLI --config behavior, layered override behavior, and tests that load legacy and v1 example configs without mutating defaults.
close_reason: Implemented: (1) Added _reject_unknown_keys validation to generator/config.py — enforces strict v1 field surface at top-level, company, and subsidiary scopes. (2) Wrote config-compatibility-v1.md policy doc covering backward compat, unknown-field rejection, versioning stance, CLI --config behavior, and layered override policy. (3) Added 10 new tests: unknown key rejection at all 3 levels, multi-key error reporting, build-time field acceptance, augmentation acceptance, optional subsidiary fields, version-less v1 loading, legacy config backward compat, no shared mutable state. All 1851 tests pass.

Combined entry point validate_augmentation_output(). All return field-level ValidationMessage results. 28 tests in test_augmentation_validators.py, full suite green (1157 passed).

title: Design cached augmentation artifact store
description: Design versioned cache keyed by scenario_hash, tool/version, model alias, prompt hash, and seed namespace so optional LLM/DataDesigner outputs do not break default byte determinism.
design:
## Cached Augmentation Artifact Store — Design

### 1. Cache Location

```
.augmentation_cache/
├── manifest.json          # Index of all cached artifacts
├── artifacts/
│   └── {artifact_hash}/   # One directory per unique (scenario, prompt, model, seed) tuple
│       ├── meta.json      # Key components, creation timestamp, model response metadata
│       └── output.json    # The cached LLM output (narrative text fragments)
```

Default location: `{project_root}/.augmentation_cache/`
Configurable via `config.yaml` → `augmentation.cache_dir` or `--cache-dir` CLI flag.
The cache directory is gitignored by default but can be committed for reproducible CI builds.

### 2. Cache Key Design

Each cached artifact is keyed by a composite hash of 5 components:

```python
cache_key = sha256(
    scenario_hash       # Hash of the ScenarioContext base seed + scenario config subset
    + tool_version      # e.g. "augmenter/1.0" — bumping invalidates all cache entries
    + model_alias       # e.g. "openrouter/nemotron-3-nano-30b" — different model = different cache
    + prompt_hash       # SHA-256 of the Jinja2 prompt template after variable substitution
    + seed_namespace    # ScenarioContext namespace path (e.g. "tc_04.lease_narratives")
)
```

**scenario_hash**: Derived from `ScenarioContext.base_seed` + a hash of the config fields that affect the canonical data feeding the prompt. This means changing the company revenue invalidates cache entries that reference revenue, but changing an unrelated config field does not.

Implementation: `ScenarioContext.child("augmentation").child_seed(namespace)` provides the seed component. The config hash is computed over the frozen `Config` dataclass fields relevant to the augmentation target (serialized via `json.dumps(asdict(config.company), sort_keys=True)`).

**tool_version**: A monotonic string embedded in the augmenter module (`__version__ = "1.0"`). Bumping this invalidates all cached artifacts, serving as a manual cache-bust when prompt engineering changes the augmentation approach.

**prompt_hash**: SHA-256 of the fully-rendered prompt text (after Jinja2 substitution). This means editing the prompt template automatically invalidates affected cache entries without bumping tool_version.

**model_alias**: The model identifier string from config (`augmentation.model`). Different models produce different text, so they get separate cache entries.

**seed_namespace**: The ScenarioContext namespace path used for this augmentation call. This ties the cache entry to the specific data context (e.g., TC-04 lease narratives vs TC-16 engagement letter prose).

### 3. Cache Manifest Integration

The cache manifest (`manifest.json`) maps artifact hashes to metadata:

```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "tool_version": "1.0",
  "entries": {
    "a1b2c3d4e5f6...": {
      "scenario_hash": "...",
      "model_alias": "openrouter/nemotron-3-nano-30b",
      "prompt_hash": "...",
      "seed_namespace": "tc_04.lease_narratives",
      "created_at": "2026-04-11T12:00:00Z",
      "token_usage": {"input": 138, "output": 432},
      "artifact_path": "artifacts/a1b2c3d4e5f6.../output.json"
    }
  }
}
```

The main generator `Manifest` (manifest.py) gains an optional `augmentation_source` field on `ManifestEntry`:
- `null` (default): file was generated without augmentation
- `"cache:{artifact_hash}"`: file used cached augmentation artifact
- `"baseline"`: augmentation was enabled but cache miss, fell back to template text

This lets the auto_grader distinguish augmented vs baseline outputs without inspecting file contents.

### 4. Cache Invalidation Rules

| Trigger | Effect |
|---------|--------|
| `tool_version` bump | All cache entries invalidated (full rebuild) |
| Prompt template edit | Only entries with that prompt_hash invalidated |
| Model change | Only entries with that model_alias invalidated |
| Config change (company fields) | Entries whose scenario_hash depended on changed fields invalidated |
| Seed change | All entries invalidated (different ScenarioContext base) |
| Manual: `--clear-augmentation-cache` | Deletes entire `.augmentation_cache/` |

Invalidation is lazy: stale entries are not proactively deleted. On cache lookup, the key is recomputed from current inputs. A miss means the old entry is simply ignored. A periodic `--prune-augmentation-cache` command can clean orphaned artifacts.

### 5. Disabled-by-Default Behavior

```yaml
# config.yaml
augmentation:
  enabled: false          # Default: OFF — generator produces identical output to pre-augmentation
  model: ""               # Required when enabled (e.g. "openrouter/nemotron-3-nano-30b")
  cache_dir: ".augmentation_cache"
  warm_on_miss: false     # If true, cache miss triggers live LLM call; if false, falls back to baseline
```

**Default path** (`enabled: false`):
- Generator skips all augmentation logic
- No cache reads or writes
- Output is byte-identical to pre-augmentation baseline
- No network calls, no provider dependency

**Enabled path** (`enabled: true`):
1. Generator computes cache key for each augmentation point
2. Cache hit → use cached artifact
3. Cache miss + `warm_on_miss: true` → call LLM provider, cache result, use it
4. Cache miss + `warm_on_miss: false` → fall back to template text (baseline)

**Explicit warm-up** (`--warm-augmentation-cache`):
- Iterates all augmentation points across all 18 TCs
- Computes cache keys, calls LLM for any misses
- Writes results to cache
- Does NOT modify generator output files
- Reports: total calls, tokens used, estimated cost, cache hit rate

### 6. API Surface

```python
# generator/augmentation.py (new module, ~200 lines)

class AugmentationCache:
    """Versioned cache for LLM-generated narrative artifacts."""
    
    def __init__(self, cache_dir: Path, tool_version: str, config: Config) -> None: ...
    
    def get(self, prompt: str, model_alias: str, seed_namespace: str) -> str | None:
        """Look up cached output. Returns None on miss."""
    
    def put(self, prompt: str, model_alias: str, seed_namespace: str, output: str, 
            token_usage: dict) -> str:
        """Store output in cache. Returns artifact hash."""
    
    def warm(self, prompts: list[AugmentationRequest]) -> WarmResult:
        """Warm cache for all given prompts. Calls LLM for misses only."""
    
    def prune(self) -> int:
        """Remove orphaned artifacts not referenced by current manifest. Returns count."""
    
    @property
    def manifest(self) -> dict: ...

@dataclass
class AugmentationRequest:
    prompt_template: str
    template_vars: dict
    model_alias: str
    seed_namespace: str

@dataclass  
class WarmResult:
    hits: int
    misses: int
    total_tokens: int
    estimated_cost_usd: float
```

### 7. Determinism Guarantee

With cache warm:
- All augmentation lookups are cache hits → deterministic (same bytes every run)
- Cache files are JSON with sorted keys → byte-stable across reads

With cache cold + `warm_on_miss: false`:
- Falls back to baseline template text → deterministic (existing behavior)

With cache cold + `warm_on_miss: true`:
- First run is non-deterministic (LLM outputs vary)
- Cache is written on first run
- Subsequent runs are deterministic (cache hits)
- This mode is explicitly opt-in and documented as non-deterministic on first run

The SEED=42 byte-identical quality gate applies to the default path (`augmentation.enabled: false`) and to the augmented path when cache is warm. It does NOT apply to the first-run cache-warming scenario, which is an explicit user action.
acceptance_criteria: Design covers cache location, manifest integration, invalidation, and disabled-by-default behavior.
close_reason: Design complete. Documented cache location (.augmentation_cache/), 5-component cache key (scenario_hash + tool_version + model_alias + prompt_hash + seed_namespace), manifest integration, lazy invalidation rules, disabled-by-default config, API surface (~200-line AugmentationCache class), and determinism guarantees for all three modes (disabled, cache-warm, cache-cold). Design is recorded in the bead's --design field for downstream beads (8ok.7.3 validators, 8ok.7.5 feature gate) to reference.

title: Spike DataDesigner with one canonical seed dataset
description: Run a minimal spike using canonical seed data, preferably TC-12 contracts or TC-04 lease facts, to generate auxiliary narrative text and assess dependency/provider requirements.
acceptance_criteria: Spike documents setup, output shape, determinism/caching implications, and whether to adopt DataDesigner directly or mimic its pattern.
notes:
## Spike Results (2026-04-11)

### Setup
- Package: `data-designer` v0.5.6 (pip install data-designer)
- Python 3.10-3.13 compatible (matches our 3.11+ requirement)
- Requires API key for NVIDIA, OpenAI, or OpenRouter
- Default text model: nvidia/nemotron-3-nano-30b-a3b via OpenRouter

### What Worked
- Successfully extracted 15 canonical leases from `generator.model.build` into a pandas DataFrame
- DataDesigner accepted the DataFrame as seed via `DataFrameSeedSource`
- Preview generated 2 lease narrative paragraphs in ~8 seconds via OpenRouter
- Output quality: realistic commercial lease language that faithfully preserved all canonical facts (lease_id, parties, dates, amounts, term, type)
- Jinja2 template variables in prompts ({{ field }}) work correctly for referencing seed columns

### Output Shape
- Produces tabular data (DataFrames): one row per seed record + LLM-generated columns
- Per-row text output, not per-document — would need post-processing to embed into PDFs/DOCX
- Token usage: ~138 input + 432 output tokens per record

### Determinism Assessment
- LLM outputs are inherently stochastic (temperature=1.0, top_p=1.0 by default)
- No built-in output caching — every preview/create call hits the LLM provider
- To meet SEED=42 byte-identical requirement: must build external cache layer
- Cache key: (prompt_hash, model_id, seed_data_hash, config_hash)

### Dependency Footprint
- data-designer pulls ~50+ transitive packages including: pydantic, httpx, tiktoken, scipy, sqlfluff, uvicorn, starlette, rich, typer
- Not compatible with locked stack (synth-data-1l0.26)
- Runtime requires network access (violates offline generation rule)

### Recommendation: MIMIC THE PATTERN
Do NOT adopt DataDesigner directly. Instead build a thin augmentation layer (~200 lines) that:
1. Takes canonical facts as seed (same concept)
2. Calls any OpenAI-compatible API for narrative text generation
3. Caches outputs keyed by (prompt_hash, model, seed_hash)
4. Falls back to template text when cache is cold (preserving offline default)
5. Integrates directly with existing formatter pipeline

Rationale:
- 50+ new dependencies vs 1 (httpx, already transitively available)
- Full control over caching, determinism, and document-oriented output format
- DataDesigner's pipeline/scale features are overkill for ~50-100 narrative snippets
- Our formatters already embed text into PDFs/DOCX — we just need better text, not a new pipeline
close_reason: Spike complete. DataDesigner v0.5.6 successfully generated realistic lease narratives from canonical seed data via OpenRouter. Output quality is good (preserves all canonical facts), but direct adoption is not recommended: 50+ transitive deps, no built-in caching, requires live LLM provider at generation time. Recommendation: mimic the pattern with a thin ~200-line augmentation layer using httpx + local cache, keeping default generation offline and deterministic.

title: Choose terminal UI framework and dependency strategy
description:
Compare Textual, prompt_toolkit, and no-new-dependency wizard for offline terminal scenario configuration, testing, dependency footprint, and UX capability.

## Alternatives Considered
1. Textual: richer TUI widgets and layout, but adds dependency and test-surface complexity.
2. prompt_toolkit: lighter interactive prompts and autocompletion, but less structure for multi-screen editing flows.
3. No-new-dependency wizard: lowest dependency risk and easiest offline compatibility, but weaker editing ergonomics and less room for validation/navigation UI.

## Decision Criteria
Pick the smallest approach that can support field navigation, validation errors, preview/diff, atomic save confirmation, and headless-friendly smoke tests without making normal generation depend on TUI-only packages.
design:
# Decision: Use Textual for TUI Framework

## Selected Path
**Textual** (textualize/textual) as an optional dependency in `[project.optional-dependencies.tui]`.

## Rationale
The TUI epic requires multi-screen editing flows (company profile → difficulty → TC overrides → validation errors → diff preview → atomic save), validation error panels with field-path navigation, and merged config preview with diff highlighting. These are layout-intensive features that require widget composition, not just sequential prompts.

Textual provides:
- **Screen/widget composition**: native multi-screen navigation with Header, Footer, DataTable, Tree, Input, Static, etc.
- **Built-in testing**: `pilot` framework for headless interaction tests — satisfies the "headless-friendly smoke tests" requirement without mocking stdin/stdout.
- **CSS-like styling**: layout and theming without manual curses positioning.
- **Async-first**: non-blocking validation and preview rendering.

## Alternatives Considered

### prompt_toolkit
- (+) Lighter than Textual, mature, good autocomplete
- (-) No layout engine — multi-screen navigation, validation panels, and diff views require significant custom scaffolding
- (-) Testing requires manual stdin/stdout mocking
- **Rejected**: too much custom layout code needed for the required UX

### No-new-dependency wizard (input/curses)
- (+) Zero dependency risk, simplest offline story
- (-) Field navigation, scrollable validation panels, and diff preview are extremely tedious in raw curses
- (-) Testing is hardest — must mock terminal I/O manually
- (-) Would likely result in a worse UX that discourages use
- **Rejected**: effort/quality tradeoff is poor for the required feature set

## Dependency Strategy
- Textual goes in `[project.optional-dependencies]` as `tui = ["textual>=0.89,<1"]`
- The TUI entry point (`generate_test_suite.py configure` or `python -m generator.tui`) does a lazy import with a clear error message if textual is not installed
- Core generation path (`generate_test_suite.py --output ...`) NEVER imports textual
- This means `uv pip install synth-data` works without textual; `uv pip install synth-data[tui]` adds it

## Testing Approach
- Use Textual's `pilot` for headless smoke tests (import, navigate screens, verify widgets render)
- Service layer (load/validate/merge/diff/save) is tested independently with pytest — no TUI dependency needed
- TUI tests go in `tests/test_tui/` and are skipped if textual is not installed (`pytest.importorskip("textual")`)

## Risk Mitigation
- Optional dependency keeps the generation CLI clean and the locked stack intact
- If Textual proves problematic, the service layer (synth-data-2u6.6.3) is framework-agnostic — the TUI screens are a thin presentation layer on top of it
- Version pinned to `<1` to avoid breaking changes from a future Textual 1.0

title: Define layered customization schema and merge semantics
description:
Design preset/company_profile/industry_profile/difficulty_profile/test_case_overrides/output_profile/error_profile layers, merge precedence, validation timing, and v1 supported fields.

## Decision
Define a layered customization schema with explicit merge order, deterministic seed behavior, a bounded v1 supported-field surface, and a documented unsupported-field policy before implementing config dataclasses or UI surfaces.

## Rationale
Scenario customization will drive CLI overrides, TUI editing, model construction, and documentation. Without a single schema decision, those surfaces can drift or accidentally expose unsupported model constants as stable user-facing configuration.

## Alternatives Considered
1. Keep only the current single config.yaml path and defer layering. This avoids schema design work but does not support reusable presets or non-destructive overrides.
2. Allow arbitrary override keys mapped directly to model constants. This is flexible but makes deterministic invariants, validation, and future migration harder.
3. Define a small v1 surface and reject unsupported fields. This is the preferred path because it keeps customization useful while protecting determinism and compatibility.
design:
# Layered Customization Schema — v1 Design

## Layer Stack (merge order, last wins)

Layers are merged bottom-to-top. Each layer is an optional YAML file. The generator loads them in this order and deep-merges, with later layers overriding earlier ones at the leaf-key level.

```
1. defaults        (hardcoded in code — the current config.yaml values)
2. preset           (--preset=<name>  → presets/<name>.yaml)
3. company_profile  (--company=<path> → user-supplied company YAML)
4. industry_profile (--industry=<name> → presets/industries/<name>.yaml)
5. difficulty_profile (--difficulty=easy|medium|hard|adversarial)
6. output_profile   (--output=<path>  → controls which TCs to emit, formats)
7. error_profile    (--errors=<path>  → override planted error selection/density)
8. test_case_overrides (--tc-override=<path> → per-TC parameter tweaks)
9. CLI flags        (--set key=value  → leaf-level override from command line)
```

## Merge Semantics

- **Deep merge**: dicts are recursively merged, not replaced. This means `company.subsidiaries.precision_components.revenue: 50_000_000` in a layer only overrides that one field.
- **List replace**: lists (e.g., `company.years`, `company.employees.remote_states`) are replaced wholesale, not appended. This avoids ambiguity about ordering and deduplication.
- **Null deletes**: setting a key to `null` in a higher layer removes it from the merged result. This allows a preset to remove a subsidiary, for example.
- **Type preservation**: a layer cannot change a field's type (int → str). The validator rejects type mismatches after merge.

## Seed Behavior

- `seed` is always present in the final merged config (defaults to 42).
- Each layer can set `seed`, but it's leaf-level — it doesn't compose.
- The seed drives ALL randomness: Faker, numpy, random, and canary generation.
- A preset that changes company parameters but keeps seed=42 will produce different data (because the data model depends on company params), but the generation process remains deterministic.
- Changing the seed changes everything. This is by design — there is no per-subsystem seed isolation in v1.

## v1 Supported Fields (safe to customize)

These fields can be overridden in any layer without breaking generator invariants:

### Company Profile
- `company.name` (str)
- `company.type` (str)
- `company.industry` (str)
- `company.headquarters` (str)
- `company.fiscal_year_end` (str, MM-DD)
- `company.years` (list[int], 3 consecutive)
- `company.current_year` (int, must be max of years)
- `company.consolidated_revenue` (int, >0)

### Subsidiary Structure
- `company.subsidiaries.*` (full subsidiary dicts)
- Adding/removing subsidiaries is allowed; minimum 1
- Each subsidiary must have all required fields

### Financial Parameters
- `company.growth_rates.*` (floats, any reasonable value)
- `company.intercompany.*` (floats/ints)
- `company.employees.*` (int/float/list)
- `company.seasonal_weights.*` (floats, must sum to 1.0)

### Generator Control
- `seed` (int)
- `output_dir` (str)

### Output Profile (new)
- `output.enabled_test_cases` (list[str], e.g. ["TC-01", "TC-06", "TC-09"])
- `output.enabled_packs` (list[str], e.g. ["accounting", "legal_hr_diligence"])
- `output.formats` (reserved for v2 — currently always xlsx/docx/pdf per TC spec)

### Difficulty Profile (new)
- `difficulty.error_density` (float, 0.0–1.0, fraction of possible errors to inject)
- `difficulty.canary_visibility` (str: "visible"|"subtle"|"hidden")
- `difficulty.judgment_trap_density` (float, 0.0–1.0)

### Error Profile (new)
- `errors.include` (list[str], error IDs to always inject)
- `errors.exclude` (list[str], error IDs to never inject)
- `errors.density_override` (float, overrides difficulty.error_density)

## v1 Unsupported Fields (reject with clear error)

These fields are internal to the generator and must NOT be exposed as customizable. If a user tries to set them, the validator emits a clear error explaining why:

- `canary_assignments` — computed from seed, not user-settable
- `error_injections` — derived from error_registry.json + error_profile
- `company.subsidiaries.*.entity_code` — used as join keys across TCs; changing them breaks cross-referential integrity
- Any field not listed in v1 supported — reject with "unsupported in v1, file a feature request"

## Validation Timing

1. **Per-layer**: each layer file is validated for YAML syntax and type correctness before merge.
2. **Post-merge**: the merged config is validated for semantic constraints (seasonal weights sum, subsidiary count, year ordering, etc.).
3. **Pre-generation**: the final Config dataclass runs __post_init__ checks (existing behavior).

Validation errors reference the layer that introduced the bad value, not just the merged result.

## Example: Merged Scenario

Given:
- defaults: Cascade Industries, seed=42, $200M revenue
- preset: `presets/small-company.yaml` → revenue=50M, 1 subsidiary, seed=42
- difficulty: `--difficulty=easy` → error_density=0.3, canary_visibility=visible
- CLI: `--set company.name="Acme Corp"`

Result:
```yaml
seed: 42
company:
  name: "Acme Corp"  # from CLI
  consolidated_revenue: 50_000_000  # from preset
  subsidiaries:  # from preset (1 subsidiary)
    main:
      legal_name: "Acme Main LLC"
      ...
difficulty:
  error_density: 0.3  # from difficulty profile
  canary_visibility: "visible"
```

## File Layout

```
presets/
├── default.yaml           # The current config.yaml, reorganized
├── small-company.yaml     # 1 subsidiary, ~$50M
├── large-conglomerate.yaml  # 5+ subsidiaries, ~$2B
└── industries/
    ├── healthcare.yaml
    ├── technology.yaml
    └── financial-services.yaml
```

## Migration Path

The current `config.yaml` becomes the defaults layer. Existing behavior is preserved when no layers are specified — the generator produces identical output to today. This is the backward-compatibility contract.

title: Three model RNG generators share identical seed (rd_time, rd_supply, ap)
description: generator/model/build.py:230-233 — rd_time_rng, rd_supply_rng, and ap_rng are all seeded with effective_seed (no offset). Bank model correctly uses effective_seed+2. Fix: Use different offsets (e.g., effective_seed+3, +4, +5) or ScenarioContext namespacing.

title: Fix TC-16 fee schedule PDF clipping
description:
## Problem
Visual inspection of the generated all-pack suite at /tmp/synth-data-generated-docs-20260413-1 found that both TC-16 fee schedule workbooks render with truncated note text when converted to PDF via LibreOffice. The affected files are test_cases/TC-16/input_files/fee_schedule.xlsx and test_cases/TC-16-EU/input_files/fee_schedule_eu.xlsx. In the first-page renders, the complexity multiplier / explanatory note text is cut off at the right edge, making the fee schedule look incomplete even though the workbook itself is structurally readable.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Generate the all-pack suite: uv run python generate_test_suite.py --packs all --output /tmp/synth-data-generated-docs-20260413-1
2. Convert test_cases/TC-16/input_files/fee_schedule.xlsx and test_cases/TC-16-EU/input_files/fee_schedule_eu.xlsx to PDF with LibreOffice headless.
3. Render the first page of each converted PDF with pdftoppm or open them visually.
4. Observe that the explanatory notes to the right of the complexity multiplier rows are truncated at the page edge.

## Expected
Both fee schedule workbooks render to PDF with complete, readable explanatory note text and no right-edge clipping.
acceptance_criteria: Regenerate TC-16 and TC-16-EU and confirm fee_schedule.xlsx and fee_schedule_eu.xlsx render to PDF with complete, readable note text and no right-edge clipping. Prefer adjusting column widths, wrapping, print area, or page orientation rather than relying on consumers to open the workbook manually.
notes: Classification review 2026-04-13: Treat as a test-case/generator-quality issue, not an evaluated-agent responsibility. The evaluated agent may read the workbook cells, but the generated professional-services document should not render explanatory fee-schedule notes clipped at the page edge unless that clipping is intentionally documented as a robustness challenge. Current bead acceptance should remain fix-oriented.
close_reason: Fixed PDF clipping in TC-16 and TC-16-EU fee schedules: added wrap_text alignment on complexity multiplier and per-entity adder note cells, widened columns A and C, added landscape orientation with fit-to-width page setup. All 121 tests pass, determinism verified.

title: Define formatter-family matrix for controlled document noise
description:
Define the formatter-family coverage matrix for controlled document noise before expanding beyond the pilot.

The matrix should identify supported output families, representative formatters, canary-placement patterns, and any intentionally excluded families with rationale.
design:
# Formatter-Family Coverage Matrix

## Supported Families

| Family | Library | Representative TCs/Files | Canary Placement | Noise Priority |
|--------|---------|--------------------------|------------------|----------------|
| **XLSX** | openpyxl | TC-01 (TB), TC-02 (GL cash), TC-03 (revenue analytics), TC-06 (provision), TC-09 (interco), TC-10 (apportion), TC-11 (P&L), TC-12 (fin stmts) | Document properties → description | **P1 — pilot** |
| **CSV** | stdlib csv | TC-02 (bank stmt, 340 rows), TC-08 (time records, 2340 rows), TC-13 (AP txns, 52K rows) | First-line comment: \`# CANARY: {code}\` | **P1 — pilot** |
| **DOCX** | python-docx | TC-03 (mgmt rep letter), TC-05 (AR memo), TC-06 (tax docs), TC-08 (R&D memos), TC-10 (rules ref), TC-11 (findings memo), TC-12 (DD memo) | Core properties → comments | **P1 — pilot** |
| **PDF (text-native)** | reportlab | TC-01 (fin stmts), TC-02 (bank confirm), TC-03 (industry benchmark), TC-04 (10 leases), TC-09 (TP report, 42pp), TC-11 (contracts), TC-12 (data room) | PDF metadata → Author | **P2 — second wave** |
| **PDF (scanned-style)** | fpdf2 | TC-04 (3 scanned leases w/ OCR traps), TC-07 (5 K-1 PDFs), templates (formatting guide) | PDF metadata → Subject | **P2 — second wave** |

## Excluded Families (with rationale)

| Family | Rationale |
|--------|-----------|
| **JSON** (registry files) | canary_registry.json and error_registry.json are machine-generated control files, not test-case inputs. Noise would corrupt the grading pipeline. Excluded permanently. |
| **Markdown / plain text** | prompt.md and expected_behavior.md are specification docs, not generated data. No noise applicable. |
| **Images** (standalone PNG/JPG) | No test case currently generates standalone image files. If added later, treat as new family and revisit. |

## Noise Dimensions per Family

### XLSX (pilot)
- **Date formats**: Mixed MM/DD/YYYY vs DD-MMM-YY vs serial dates within a single workbook
- **Header perturbation**: Extra whitespace, inconsistent casing (e.g., "Account Name" vs "account_name" vs "ACCOUNT NAME")
- **Number formatting**: Some cells stored as text-formatted numbers (leading apostrophe pattern)
- **Empty rows/columns**: Spurious blank rows between data sections
- **Merged cells**: Occasional merged header cells that break naive column indexing
- **Risk — canary preservation**: Canary is in document properties (metadata). Noise targets cell content and formatting only. No conflict.
- **Risk — planted error preservation**: Errors are in specific cell values. Noise must not touch cells listed in error_registry.json.

### CSV (pilot)
- **Encoding variation**: UTF-8-BOM vs plain UTF-8 (BOM confuses naive readers)
- **Delimiter ambiguity**: Quoted fields containing commas; occasional trailing comma on header row
- **Whitespace**: Leading/trailing spaces in field values
- **Missing headers**: One CSV variant with data starting on row 1 (no header)
- **Risk — canary preservation**: Canary is line 1 comment. Noise starts from line 2+. No conflict.
- **Risk — planted error preservation**: Errors reference specific row/column positions. Noise must not alter rows containing planted errors.

### DOCX (pilot)
- **Style inconsistency**: Mixed font sizes within body text (11pt vs 12pt), orphaned bold/italic runs
- **Paragraph formatting**: Inconsistent line spacing (single vs 1.15 vs 1.5)
- **Table formatting**: Missing borders on some table cells, inconsistent column widths
- **Metadata remnants**: Track-changes author name left in revisions (not visible but parseable)
- **Risk — canary preservation**: Canary is in core properties → comments. Noise targets document body. No conflict.
- **Risk — planted error preservation**: Errors reference specific text content. Noise must not alter paragraphs containing planted error strings.

### PDF text-native (second wave)
- **Font substitution**: Occasional Helvetica→Arial or Times→Times New Roman swap across pages
- **Whitespace irregularity**: Non-breaking spaces mixed with regular spaces in numeric columns
- **Page numbering**: Inconsistent format ("Page 1 of 10" vs "1" vs "- 1 -")
- **Risk — canary preservation**: Canary is in Author metadata. Noise targets page content. No conflict.
- **Risk — planted error preservation**: Errors are in specific text content. Noise must avoid altering error-bearing text runs.

### PDF scanned-style (second wave)
- **Already noisy**: TC-04 scanned leases include OCR traps ($→S, 1→l). Additional noise must not compound to make extraction impossible.
- **Image quality**: Slight rotation (0.5°–1°), faint watermark, uneven margins
- **Approach**: Limit to layout perturbation only; OCR-character noise is already covered by existing formatters.
- **Risk — canary preservation**: Canary in Subject metadata (not in image content). Safe.
- **Risk — planted error preservation**: Errors are in text content rendered as images. Layout noise (rotation, margins) does not alter text. Safe.

## Implementation Guidance for synth-data-8ok.5

1. **Pilot scope**: Implement noise helpers for XLSX, CSV, and DOCX (3 families). Pick one representative TC per family as proof-of-concept:
   - XLSX → TC-01 (trial balance — highest coverage, 2 xlsx outputs)
   - CSV → TC-02 (bank statement — moderate row count, clear structure)
   - DOCX → TC-05 (AR workpaper memo — standalone, minimal cross-references)

2. **Guard rails**: The noise profile must accept an exclusion list of (file, cell/row/paragraph) tuples derived from error_registry.json. No noise may touch a planted-error location or a canary-bearing metadata field.

3. **Determinism**: All noise must be seeded from the ScenarioContext namespace (synth-data-ln9.1). Use a dedicated sub-namespace like `noise:{tc_id}:{file_key}` to avoid seed collision with data generation.

4. **Second wave**: After pilot validation, extend to PDF text-native and PDF scanned-style. The scanned-style family requires special care — only layout noise, no additional OCR-character confusion.

5. **Evaluation**: After pilot, run the auto_grader against a noise-enabled suite. If any TC score degrades vs. the clean suite, the noise is too aggressive for that family and must be tuned down.
acceptance_criteria: Matrix covers each supported formatter family or explicitly justifies exclusion; TC/file representatives are named; risks for xlsx, csv, docx, pdf, and generated narrative/document outputs are called out; synth-data-8ok.5 acceptance can be evaluated against the matrix.
close_reason: Defined formatter-family matrix in docs/formatter-family-matrix.yaml. Identifies 5 noise-eligible families (xlsx_openpyxl, csv_stdlib, docx_python_docx, pdf_reportlab, pdf_fpdf2) with canary patterns, noise surfaces, and risks. 4 families intentionally excluded (gold JSON, registries, config YAML, markdown prompts) with rationale. Representatives named for pilot validation. docx_template_copy tracked separately for merge-field safety.
